sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Apple Music to Replace Pepsi as Sponsor of NFL’s Super Bowl Halftime Show
feed.summary: Apple Music has signed a multiyear deal with the NFL to become the new sponsor of the Super Bowl halftime show, replacing PepsiCo Inc. The NFL announced the deal on Friday. The halftime show is a highly-watched, elaborate performance featuring popular musical acts, and Pepsi had been the sponsor for a decade, with its deal estimated at $2.3 billion in 2011.

feed.title: Shrugging off crypto winter, Galaxy Digital’s Mike Novogratz calls inaugural NFT launch ‘building for the future’
feed.summary: Despite the ongoing "crypto winter" and declining NFT sales, Galaxy Digital CEO Mike Novogratz remains bullish on NFTs, launching the company's inaugural collection of 3,210 digital collectibles. The NFTs, created in partnership with Time's Web3 initiative TIMEPieces, will be airdropped for free to employees and partners, with Novogratz emphasizing it as "building for the future" and a way to foster community spirit rather than a financial endeavor. He points to corporate adoption of NFTs by companies like Nike and the NBA as evidence of their long-term viability.

feed.title: Apple Begins Making iPhone 14 in India Weeks Ahead of Schedule
feed.summary: Apple has started manufacturing its new iPhone 14 in India earlier than expected, significantly reducing the production lag between China and India from months to mere weeks. This move aligns with Apple's strategy to diversify its supply chain amidst geopolitical tensions and disruptions in China, while also supporting India's ambition to become a major technology manufacturing hub. The accelerated timeline is a result of resolving supply chain issues and collaboration with manufacturing partner Foxconn.

feed.title: Peloton losing global marketing head to AutoDesk
feed.summary: Peloton's senior vice president and global head of marketing, communications, and membership, Dara Treseder, is leaving the company to become the chief marketing officer of AutoDesk, effective October 14. This departure follows a series of other executive resignations at Peloton and comes as the company is undergoing an $800 million restructuring plan to address significant financial losses and declining revenue. Treseder was instrumental in growing Peloton's membership base during her tenure.

feed.title: Peloton marketing, comms chief Dara Treseder joins Autodesk
feed.summary: Dara Treseder, formerly Peloton's SVP and global head of marketing, comms, and membership, has been appointed Chief Marketing Officer at Autodesk, effective October 14. She will be responsible for Autodesk's global marketing strategy, overseeing worldwide marketing, brand and communications, global demand generation, and education business teams. Treseder's appointment comes as Autodesk reported a 17% increase in revenue to $1.2 billion in Q2 of its 2023 fiscal year.

feed.title: Assurant (AIZ) Boosts Mobile Device Protection With T-Mobile
feed.summary: Assurant (AIZ) has renewed its multiyear partnership with T-Mobile, aiming to enhance mobile device protection solutions through its Global Lifestyle segment. This agreement is expected to expand service and support options, including integrating Assurant's Cell Phone Repair (CPR) network for T-Mobile's device repair program starting in October. The extension reinforces Assurant's strategy to grow its Connected Living platform and follows similar partnerships with other major brands.

feed.title: 삼성자산운용, 월배당 'DIVO ETF' 한국판 출시
feed.summary: 삼성자산운용이 미국 운용사 앰플리파이의 월배당 커버드콜 ETF인 'DIVO'의 한국판 상품을 출시한다. 이 상품은 'KODEX 미국 배당 프리미엄 액티브 ETF'로, 미국 우량 배당 성장주와 대표 성장주에 투자하며 주식 배당과 콜옵션 매도를 통해 월 배당 재원을 마련한다. 특히 개별 주식에 대한 전략적인 콜옵션 매도와 자산의 주가 상승에 따른 자본 이익 추구하는 액티브 전략이 특징이다.

feed.title: Apple and Globalstar Deal May Signal Future of Personal Satellite Communications
feed.summary: Apple's new iPhone 14 features satellite connectivity through a deal with Globalstar, initially for emergency distress messages. This partnership highlights a burgeoning market for smartphone-to-satellite communications, with companies like Lynk, AST SpaceMobile, and Starlink (with T-Mobile) also developing similar services. The article explores how this technology, while currently limited to basic functions like emergency SOS or text, represents a significant shift in personal communications and creates new opportunities for the space industry, including Canadian firms like MDA.

feed.title: Vornado Realty Trust, Ironworkers, Turner, Skanska and partners celebrate topping out of 88,000 s/f The Bustle at PENN 2
feed.summary: Vornado Realty Trust and its partners, including Ironworkers, Turner, and Skanska, celebrated the topping out of "The Bustle" at PENN 2. This 88,000 s/f space will serve as a reimagined entrance to Penn Station, featuring a triple-height lobby, public art, and direct access to the station. The project emphasizes sustainability, with facade replacements reducing thermal transmission and carbon emissions, and will relocate PENN 2's entrance to the new 33rd St. Plaza.

feed.title: SK Telecom, KT raise Galaxy Z Flip 4 subsidies ahead of iPhone 14 launch
feed.summary: South Korean mobile carriers SK Telecom and KT Corp. have increased subsidies for the 512-gigabyte model of Samsung's Galaxy Z Flip 4. This move comes ahead of the iPhone 14 launch next month, as the carriers aim to strengthen the competitiveness of Samsung's foldable smartphone amidst anticipated competition from Apple. LG Uplus, the third-largest carrier, did not adjust its subsidy program.

feed.title: SK Telecom, KT raise Galaxy Z Flip 4 subsidies ahead of iPhone 14 launch
feed.summary: Korean mobile carriers SK Telecom and KT have increased subsidies for the 512-gigabyte model of Samsung's Galaxy Z Flip 4 in anticipation of the iPhone 14 launch. This move aims to bolster the competitiveness of Samsung's foldable smartphone amidst expected competition from Apple's new models. Meanwhile, LG Uplus has not altered its subsidy program for the device.

feed.title: How Arm conquered the chip market without making a single chip, with CEO Rene Haas
feed.summary: In this interview, Arm CEO Rene Haas discusses how Arm has become a central player in the tech ecosystem by designing chip instruction sets without manufacturing chips themselves. He explains Arm's licensing and royalty business model, its broad customer base including giants like Apple and Qualcomm, and how it is diversifying into cloud, automotive, and IoT markets. Haas also addresses the company's decision to go public, its role in navigating the chip shortage, and its competitive stance against x86 and RISC-V architectures.

feed.title: Amazon.com unveils device that tracks breath while sleeping
feed.summary: Amazon has announced a new contactless sleep-tracking gadget called Halo Rise, priced at $139.99, which monitors room conditions and breathing patterns to provide insights into sleep quality. This device, not FDA-approved, is part of Amazon's broader investment in consumer health technology. The company also unveiled updates to its Kindle e-readers and voice-controlled devices.

feed.title: Pay boosts for Oracle's Ellison, Catz move their rewards past Apple's Tim Cook
feed.summary: Oracle co-founder Larry Ellison and CEO Safra Catz each received over $138 million in total compensation for the company’s fiscal year ending May 31. This pay package surpasses the compensation of Apple CEO Tim Cook. The compensation details were disclosed in Oracle's recent regulatory filing.

feed.title: Logitech Unveils Diverse Portfolio of Mice and Keyboards “Designed for Mac”
feed.summary: Logitech announced a new "Designed for Mac" collection, featuring the MX Mechanical Mini for Mac keyboard, MX Master 3S for Mac mouse, Lift for Mac mouse, and K380 Bluetooth Keyboard for Mac. These products are crafted for seamless compatibility and reliability with Mac setups, offering enhanced functionality, customization, and comfort. The collection also emphasizes sustainability by incorporating certified post-consumer recycled plastic in several products.

feed.title: Apple's $310 mln iPhone throttling settlement must be reconsidered - court
feed.summary: A federal judge has been ordered to reconsider approval of Apple's $310 million settlement in a class-action lawsuit alleging the company slowed down certain iPhones. The 9th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals found that the judge incorrectly presumed the settlement was fair without class certification and also needs to re-evaluate the $80.6 million awarded in lawyers' fees. Apple had settled the case without admitting wrongdoing, claiming the feature was for performance management.

feed.title: Fast Company shuts website after hack sends 'obscene' Apple News notifications
feed.summary: Fast Company temporarily shut down its website and Apple News feed after hackers sent "obscene and racist" push notifications to Apple users. The attack involved compromising the publication's content management system, leading to a 404 error on its website and Apple News disabling the channel. This incident followed an "apparently related" hack on Fast Company's homepage just days prior.

feed.title: Fostering Innovation Through Partnership to Achieve Net Zero
feed.summary: Prologis has committed to achieving net-zero emissions across its value chain by 2040 and joined MIT’s Climate and Sustainability Consortium (MCSC) to accelerate this goal. The partnership will focus on developing innovative solutions for challenges like concrete and steel decarbonization and low-carbon mobility. Prologis aims to leverage the MCSC's research and collaboration to inspire transformative change within the industry and advance its sustainability objectives.

feed.title: Peloton Strikes First Brick-and-Mortar Deal With Dick’s
feed.summary: Peloton has partnered with Dick's Sporting Goods, the largest sports goods retailer in the U.S., to sell its equipment in over 100 stores. This marks Peloton's first brick-and-mortar retail deal outside its own stores, following a recent partnership with Amazon. The move is part of Peloton's strategy to rebound after a pandemic-driven surge in demand cooled, leading to job cuts, reduced retail presence, and price increases for some products.

feed.title: Microsoft Corp. stock outperforms market despite losses on the day
feed.summary: Microsoft Corp. (MSFT) shares decreased by 1.48% to $237.50 on Thursday, still outperforming the broader market which saw the S&P 500 fall by 2.11% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average fall by 1.54%. The stock closed significantly below its 52-week high, but performed better than some competitors like Apple, Alphabet, and SAP SE ADR. Trading volume for Microsoft exceeded its 50-day average.

feed.title: Micron warns of tougher times, plans to cut investments by 30%
feed.summary: Micron Technology announced it would cut its investments by over 30% to around $8 billion for fiscal 2023, signaling tougher times ahead due to falling demand for PCs and smartphones. The company, which was the first major chipmaker to warn of declining demand earlier in the year, expects strong revenue growth in the second half of fiscal 2023 as demand recovers early next year. Despite grim short-term profit and revenue forecasts, analysts noted that production and capital expenditure cuts often signal that memory markets are approaching their lowest point.

feed.title: Cash App, Walmart, iPhone 14, Apple, and Intuit — Top Scams and Phishing Schemes of the Week
feed.summary: This article highlights prevalent scams impersonating major brands like Cash App, Walmart, Apple, and Intuit. Scammers use phishing links in emails and text messages to lure victims into fake online surveys or login pages, aiming to steal personal information and financial details. The article emphasizes the importance of verifying URLs, sender addresses, and avoiding unknown links, while also promoting Trend Micro's security tools as protection against these threats.

feed.title: Today Is the Last Day to File a Claim in Capital One's $190 Million Data Breach Settlement
feed.summary: Today marks the final day for Capital One customers to file a claim in the $190 million settlement resulting from a 2019 data breach that exposed personal information of over 100 million people. Eligible customers can claim up to $25,000 for lost time and expenses, along with three years of free identity protection services. The settlement stems from a class-action lawsuit arguing Capital One failed to implement adequate cybersecurity measures.

feed.title: SoftBank’s Son expected to meet Samsung's Lee for Arm M&A discussion
feed.summary: SoftBank Group CEO Masayoshi Son is anticipated to meet with Samsung Electronics de facto leader Jay Y. Lee to discuss a potential sale or strategic alliance for British chip designer Arm. This meeting follows a collapsed deal to sell Arm to Nvidia due to regulatory hurdles and a broader industry consensus that a single company acquisition might be difficult. Various scenarios are being considered, including a consortium of chipmakers or a partial acquisition by Samsung, though some industry watchers express skepticism about significant outcomes given Arm's high valuation and SoftBank's financial state.

feed.title: LANDSEA HOMES' FÖRENA, IN HEART OF NEW YORK CITY, IS 90 PERCENT SOLD
feed.summary: Landsea Homes Corporation and DNA Development announced that FÖRENA, their Scandinavian-inspired residential building in downtown Manhattan, is 90 percent sold, with fewer than five luxury residences remaining. The building, located at 540 Sixth Avenue, offers 50 residences designed by Morris Adjmi Architects, featuring high-quality finishes and generous layouts. Amenities include a Zen Garden, landscaped rooftop terrace, residents' lounge, and fitness center.

feed.title: TSMC remains world’s largest contract chipmaker
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) maintained its position as the world's largest contract chipmaker in Q2, holding a 53.4% market share despite a slight dip from the previous quarter. The company recorded $18.15 billion in sales due to demand from high-performance computing, IoT, and automotive electronics. South Korea's Samsung Electronics and Taiwan's United Microelectronics Corp followed with significant market shares.

feed.title: Interview] Jonathon Wright – Chief Technology Evangelist – Keysight Technologies
feed.summary: This interview features Jonathon Wright, Chief Technology Evangelist at Keysight Technologies, who discusses his 25 years of experience in test automation and AI. He highlights Keysight's Eggplant AI-driven solutions, which provide continuous testing for retail systems, devices, and omnichannel experiences, and explains how this technology helps retailers reduce time-to-market and improve user experiences. Wright also touches upon future retail trends like immersive shopping experiences and the metaverse, and how Keysight is positioned to support these advancements.

feed.title: China’s $5 trillion rout causes historic gap with Indian stocks
feed.summary: China's stock market has experienced a $5 trillion rout since early 2021, creating a historic divergence with the Indian stock market. The MSCI India Index rallied almost 10% in the last quarter, while the MSCI China Index slumped 23%, marking the biggest outperformance by India since March 2000. This shift is attributed to China's "Covid Zero" policy, regulatory crackdowns, and geopolitical tensions, making India an attractive alternative for investors due to its faster economic growth forecast and expanding domestic market.

feed.title: InterDigital inks seven-year, $938M patent deal with Apple
feed.summary: InterDigital has renewed its patent license agreement with Apple for seven years, a deal expected to generate $134 million annually for a total of $938 million. This agreement comes as InterDigital's deal with Samsung is also up for renewal and amidst Apple's ongoing patent licensing battles with Ericsson and potential negotiations with Nokia. The renewal highlights Apple's continued engagement in patent licensing and the growing importance of high-end technologies in connected devices.

feed.title: Spotify Technology S.A. – Leading global audio platform with pricing power
feed.summary: This report initiates coverage on Spotify Technology S.A. with a BUY recommendation and a target price of US$117.00. The company is highlighted as a leading global audio platform with significant pricing power, supported by a growing user base, increasing ARPU, and a recurring revenue business model. Future revenue growth is expected to be driven by subscriber conversions, price increases, and expanding higher-margin advertising revenue from podcasts and audiobooks.

feed.title: App Store experienced sharp revenue drop in September, Morgan Stanley says
feed.summary: Apple's App Store experienced its steepest year-on-year net revenue decline in September, dropping 5% according to Morgan Stanley analyst Erik Woodring, largely due to a 14% plunge in gaming revenue. Sensor Tower data supports this, showing Apple's September revenue at $6.9 billion, down from $7.2 billion last year, and Morgan Stanley attributes this trend to consumers reallocating discretionary income amidst a global economic downturn. Despite a predicted potential rebound in the December quarter due to an extra selling week and currency fluctuations, Apple's services revenue for the September-ending quarter also fell short of Wall Street expectations.

feed.title: Apple Suppliers Add Manufacturing Sites in U.S., With Focus on California
feed.summary: Apple Inc. suppliers increased their manufacturing presence in the U.S. in fiscal 2021, with 48 out of 180 suppliers establishing sites, up from 25 the previous year. Over 30 of these new sites are located in California, close to Apple's Cupertino headquarters. This shift is attributed to the impact of the pandemic and changing geopolitical landscapes on global supply chains.

feed.title: Student study habits have changed. McGraw Hill responds with the SHARPEN™ study app
feed.summary: McGraw Hill has launched SHARPEN™, a new mobile study app designed to address the evolving study habits of college students. A survey revealed that 75% of students changed their study methods since COVID-19 and often use social media for supplementary content, despite distrusting its information. SHARPEN™ offers bite-sized, engaging content in a social media-inspired format to provide trusted and convenient learning support.

feed.title: Identity Theft Monitoring Offered to Microsoft 365 Consumer Users
feed.summary: Microsoft has launched a new Identity Theft Monitoring service for its U.S. Microsoft 365 Personal and Family subscribers, offered at no extra charge. The service is largely outsourced to Experian, which provides up to $1 million in insurance for stolen funds and $100,000 for identity theft. Users will need to provide personal information to Experian, not Microsoft, to utilize the monitoring service.

feed.title: What’s up with… InterDigital, Orange, Calix
feed.summary: InterDigital announced improved Q3 financial guidance and a new 7-year licensing deal with Apple worth $938 million, sending its stock up. Orange initiated an energy-saving plan in France for the winter, while Calix appointed Michael Weening as its new CEO. The article also touches on other telecom news, including a Telstra data breach, Vodafone M&A activity, BSNL's 5G plans with TCS, and Lumen Technologies' $7.5 billion ILEC sale.

feed.title: Three Taiwanese firms join Apple suppliers list
feed.summary: Apple Inc. has added three new Taiwanese manufacturers—Taiwan Powder Technologies Co., Ko Ja (Cayman) Co., and Taiwan Surface Mounting Technology Corp.—to its supplier list in the past fiscal year. Major existing suppliers like Hon Hai Precision Industry Co., ASE Technology Holding Co., and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) remain on the list, demonstrating Taiwan's continued crucial role in Apple's supply chain. Separately, iPhone exports from India surpassed US$1 billion in the five months since April, indicating the country's growing presence in electronics manufacturing, although still significantly behind China.

feed.title: iShares Russell 3000 ETF IWV
feed.summary: This article provides details on the iShares Russell 3000 ETF (IWV), including its current price, key metrics, and how to invest in it through the eToro platform. It highlights that the ETF aims to replicate the performance of the Russell 3000 Index, encompassing major US companies like Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon. The piece also includes trending ETFs and educational resources on ETF investing.

feed.title: Sezzle or Afterpay? The Reviews Are In and There's a Clear Winner
feed.summary: The article compares Sezzle and Afterpay, two prominent buy-now-pay-later (BNPL) services, highlighting their differences in retailer availability, credit limits, and geographical presence. While both offer similar services, Sezzle appears to be favored in customer reviews for ease of use and support. The piece also discusses the general safety of BNPL solutions and the rapid evolution of the market.

feed.title: NLRB: Apple Interfered with Union Drive at NYC Store
feed.summary: The National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) has filed a complaint against Apple Inc., alleging the company illegally interfered with a unionization effort at its Manhattan World Trade Center store. The complaint, spurred by charges from the Communication Workers of America (CWA) union, accuses Apple of interrogating employees, forcing them into anti-union meetings, and prohibiting pro-union flyers in a break room. CWA Secretary-Treasurer Sara Steffens stated that the complaint would help hold Apple accountable for its anti-union activities and urged the company to uphold its stated values by collaborating with its employees.
feed.source: New York City Central Labor Council, AFL-CIO
feed.source_domain: New York City Central Labor Council, AFL-CIO

feed.title: Bumble cuts annual revenue forecast as Ukraine war, competition bite
feed.summary: Bumble Inc. has reduced its full-year revenue forecast for 2022 due to the impact of the war in Ukraine and intense competition from rivals like Match Group Inc. The company expects revenue between $920 million and $930 million, lower than previous estimates. While the Bumble app saw a surge in paying users, its Badoo app, primarily used in Western Europe, experienced a decline in users after Bumble exited Russia and Belarus.

feed.title: Southlake Town Square attracts stores that once required a drive to Dallas
feed.summary: Southlake Town Square is experiencing a significant surge in demand, attracting premier retail brands that previously required a drive to Dallas or are opening their first North Texas locations. This growth includes brands like Yeti, Aerie, Nike, Brandy Melville, and lululemon, with further openings planned well into 2023 for retailers such as Aritzia, Mizzen + Main, and Third Love. The shopping center's success is attributed to its strategic location and a focus on customer requests when making leasing decisions.

feed.title: Florian Seiche has left the (Nokia Mobile) building
feed.summary: Florian Seiche, the CEO of HMD Global (Nokia Mobile), has announced his departure from the company after six years to pursue other opportunities. His key accomplishment was bringing the Nokia brand back to the global market, achieving good sales in specific regions. The article discusses the implications of his exit for Nokia Mobile's future strategy.

feed.title: Sirius XM (SIRI) Introduces Grown Folk Jamz for R&B Fans
feed.summary: Sirius XM has launched "Grown Folk Jamz" on channel 362 as part of its Project Amplify initiative to diversify its programming with R&B, Gospel, Hip-Hop, Smooth Jazz, and Classic Soul music. This move aims to attract new subscribers and enhance content discovery, building on recent successes like increased self-pay subscribers and subscription revenue growth in Q2 2022. Despite these efforts, Sirius XM faces pressures from rising subscriber acquisition and music licensing costs, as well as competition from other streaming services like Apple, Spotify, and Alphabet.

feed.title: Some Apple workers in Australia vote to strike over pay, benefits
feed.summary: Australian Apple employees represented by the Retail and Fast Food Workers Union (RAFFWU) have voted to strike on October 18th due to stalled wage negotiations. This planned one-hour strike, involving about 150 of Apple's 4,000 Australian employees, is expected to disrupt services in at least three stores and marks Apple's first strike in the country. The union is demanding wage increases that keep pace with Australia's 7% inflation and guaranteed consecutive weekend days for workers.

feed.title: Writers workshop axed as layoffs hit Warner Bros. Television
feed.summary: Warner Bros. Discovery has implemented significant layoffs within its television division, cutting 82 current employees and 43 unfilled positions, totaling 125 headcount reductions, or 19% of the current staff. These cuts are part of a larger effort by CEO David Zaslav to achieve $3 billion in cost savings following the media mega-merger. Notably, the reorganization includes the cancellation of Stage 13 and the Warner Bros. Television Workshop program, both of which focused on supporting diverse talent, drawing criticism amidst calls for greater representation in the entertainment industry.

feed.title: Invesco Nasdaq 100 ETF: Stash This ETF Away In Your Roth IRA (NASDAQ:QQQM)
feed.summary: This article recommends investing in the Invesco Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQM) for a Roth IRA, especially given market turmoil. The author highlights why QQQM was chosen over the more common QQQ and discusses its appeal after significant price declines in quality tech stocks like Apple, Amazon, and Microsoft. The author also mentions a collaboration with Hoya Capital for an income-focused investing service.

feed.title: US to allow Samsung, SK Hynix to import to China for 1 year
feed.summary: The US has granted Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix a one-year waiver to import semiconductor equipment into their Chinese factories, temporarily easing concerns over the impact of new US export curbs. This decision aims to mitigate potential disruptions to the US semiconductor industry and global supply chains, but South Korean chipmakers remain in a precarious position between US regulations and their significant market presence in China. The long-term implications, including technology competitiveness and potential license requirements after the grace period, are still a concern for the companies involved.

feed.title: Haleon to improve accessibility for visually impaired customers
feed.summary: Haleon has partnered with Microsoft to enhance accessibility for blind and partially sighted customers using the Seeing AI app. The app allows users to scan barcodes of over 1,500 Haleon products, including Sensodyne and Centrum, to hear information like product names, ingredients, and instructions. This initiative aims to promote health inclusivity and greater independence for visually impaired individuals, with plans for global expansion and Android availability.

feed.title: Second time a charm for used gadgets
feed.summary: The market for secondhand electronic gadgets is booming in China, driven by tech-savvy and environmentally conscious youth seeking high-quality, affordable products. Companies like ATRenew and Zhuanzhuan are leading this trend by offering reliable recycling and trading platforms, addressing concerns about data privacy and transparent pricing, and expanding their services to include maintenance and new product categories. This growth aligns with China's push for a circular economy to achieve its carbon emissions reduction goals, highlighting the significant environmental benefits of reusing and recycling electronic devices.

feed.title: Inspired by Starbucks and Amazon Worker Wins, 300 T-Mobile Customer Service Staff Unionize
feed.summary: Inspired by successful unionization efforts at Starbucks and Amazon, approximately 300 T-Mobile social media customer service workers have formed an independent union, the T-Force Social Care Alliance (TSCA). Citing mass layoffs, pay cuts, and increased work pressure, the TSCA aims to secure protections, economic justice, and job security for its members. This move follows a 2019 merger with Sprint that led to significant job losses at T-Mobile, despite promises of job creation.

feed.title: TSMC cuts capex on tool delays, demand woes; cautious on outlook
feed.summary: TSMC, the world's largest contract chipmaker, has cut its 2022 capital expenditure by at least 10% and issued a cautious outlook for demand, predicting a chip downturn next year due to rising inflation and other macroeconomic factors. Despite an 80% surge in third-quarter profit, the company expects the semiconductor industry to decline in 2023, though TSMC itself anticipates a growth year. The company cited tool delivery challenges and capacity optimization as reasons for the capex reduction and stated that new U.S. export controls on advanced chip manufacturing for China would have a limited impact on its operations.

feed.title: Microsoft Office suite becomes Microsoft 365 app
feed.summary: Microsoft Corporation announced that its productivity suite, including Office.com, the Office mobile app, and the Office app for Windows, will merge into a single "Microsoft 365 app." This change, featuring a new icon and more features, will roll out gradually starting November 2022. Existing accounts, subscriptions, and files will be automatically updated without disruption, and users can continue to access core applications like Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook.

feed.title: Update: the SmartThings Hub and Matter
feed.summary: Samsung has provided an update on Matter features for the SmartThings Hub, clarifying which hub generations will support Thread and how they will interact with other Matter ecosystems. While 3rd generation hubs will gain Thread support and act as border routers, older models will require a separate border router for Thread devices. Initially, the SmartThings hubs will not function as a Zigbee bridge for other Matter environments, but Samsung is deepening integration with Google Home for multi-admin control.

feed.title: Mercedes-Benz strikes Dolby Atmos partnership with Apple Music and Universal
feed.summary: Mercedes-Benz has partnered with Universal Music Group and Apple Music to integrate Spatial Audio with Dolby Atmos into its vehicles, making them the first non-Apple devices to offer this feature natively in a car. This collaboration aims to set a new standard for in-car music quality, with UMG encouraging artists to mix music in Dolby Atmos and using Mercedes-Benz vehicles for song approval and quality assurance. Drivers with Apple Music subscriptions can now access Spatial Audio content in compatible Mercedes-Benz models.

feed.title: Apple fixes zero-day in iOS and iPadOS 15.0.2 emergency release: Detect and Prioritize Vulnerabilities using VMDR for Mobile Devices
feed.summary: Apple has released an emergency security update, iOS and iPadOS 15.0.2, to address a critical zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2021-30883) that is actively being exploited. This vulnerability allows arbitrary code execution with kernel privileges, posing a significant risk for spyware deployment. Qualys recommends immediate updates for all affected devices and offers VMDR for Mobile Devices to help detect and prioritize these vulnerabilities, enabling remote response actions to ensure device security.

feed.title: News Corp. stock up, Fox shares down as investors weigh possible merger
feed.summary: Shares of News Corp. rose while Fox Corp. shares fell after the companies announced they are exploring a potential merger, nearly a decade after their split. Investors are weighing the impact of recombining Rupert Murdoch's media empire, which would consolidate Fox's news, sports, and business channels with News Corp.'s properties like The Wall Street Journal. However, some media executives and an activist investor, Irenic Capital Management, have expressed skepticism about the strategic benefits and potential undervaluation of News Corp.'s real estate listings unit in such a merger.

feed.title: Gartner Top 10 Tech Trends For 2023: Metaverse, AI, Cloud
feed.summary: Gartner has released its top 10 technology trends for 2023, focusing on themes like optimizing resilience, scaling solutions, and pioneering new opportunities amidst economic uncertainty. The trends include the metaverse, super apps, adaptive AI, industry cloud platforms, and sustainability, emphasizing technology's role in addressing global challenges and driving business value beyond just cost savings. Each trend is accompanied by an example of its real-world application.

feed.title: Logitech Combo Touch, Slim Folio and Rugged Folio, as well as New Logitech Crayon Available for New iPad (10th Generation)
feed.summary: Logitech announced the availability of its popular keyboard cases—Combo Touch, Slim Folio, and Rugged Folio—along with the new Logitech Crayon, for the new iPad (10th generation). These accessories offer versatility, laptop-like typing, and robust protection, catering to various user preferences and usage scenarios. The Crayon digital pencil, featuring Apple Pencil technology, provides pixel-perfect precision and charges via USB-C.

feed.title: NBA 2K23 Arcade Edition Available Now Exclusively on Apple Arcade
feed.summary: NBA 2K23 Arcade Edition has launched exclusively on Apple Arcade, offering an authentic mobile basketball experience. Players receive exclusive Devin Booker Nike Kobe 4 Protro MyPLAYER gear for a limited time upon launch. The game features "The Greatest" mode with 20 all-time NBA players, official commentary, and Association Mode, and is available across multiple Apple devices.

feed.title: Adobe Advances 3D, Immersive and Metaverse Experiences With New Products
feed.summary: Adobe announced significant advancements in its Substance 3D tools, introducing new products like 3D Capture and Substance 3D Modeler to facilitate the creation of 3D and metaverse-ready immersive experiences. The company also revealed a partnership with Meta to integrate Substance 3D tools into the Quest platform, making 3D content creation more accessible. These innovations aim to support creators and brands in transitioning into new mediums, emphasizing ease of use and sustainability with a new Substance 3D Sustainability Calculator.

feed.title: スマートウォッチ「Amazfit Band 7」を着けて運動してみたら、厳しい評価に驚き　運動習慣をつけるきっかけに - Fav-Log by ITmedia
feed.summary: The article reviews the Amazfit Band 7 smartwatch, focusing on its utility for exercise and fitness tracking. The author, initially surprised by some "harsh" evaluations from the device, found it to be a catalyst for developing better exercise habits. The review covers the watch's 120 sports modes, automatic exercise recognition, and the detailed data provided through the accompanying Zepp app, highlighting its potential to support new and experienced exercisers alike.

feed.title: The 5x Spike & Now Implosion of Generac’s Stock Shows What Went Horribly Wrong in the Markets since Feb. 2020
feed.summary: This article by Wolf Richter on Wolf Street examines the implosion of Generac Holdings (GNRC) stock, which surged five-fold from February 2020 to November 2021 before dropping 79% from its peak. Richter uses Generac as a case study to demonstrate the "stock-market craziness" fueled by factors like ZIRP and QE policies, and the subsequent brutal unwinding of these inflated valuations. The piece also criticizes Wall Street analysts for maintaining "buy" ratings even after significant stock drops, and discusses the broader implications for dip-buyers and the market as a whole.

feed.title: New Sony-Honda Venture to Compete in Premium EVs with Software Prowess
feed.summary: Sony Honda Mobility, a new venture between Sony and Honda, announced plans to deliver high-end electric vehicles (EVs) by 2026. The company aims to differentiate itself in the competitive luxury EV market by focusing on software, offering advanced features like automation, personalization, and metaverse connectivity. Despite being a latecomer, Sony Honda Mobility seeks to challenge established players like Tesla and European automakers by leveraging Sony's sensor technology and collaborative partnerships.

feed.title: Despite late entry, analysts bullish on Spotify's audiobook play
feed.summary: Analysts are optimistic about Spotify's recent entry into the audiobook market, despite its late start. Although Spotify's audiobook store, launched after acquiring Findaway World LLC, currently has limitations like web-only purchases and U.S.-only availability, experts believe the company can leverage its success in podcasts and the overall growth of audiobooks to compete with established players like Audible. However, challenges remain, including complex licensing for audiobooks compared to podcasts and a flattening growth curve after the pandemic boom, along with Spotify's continued unprofitability despite revenue growth.

feed.title: Could refusing to return to office mean a layoff? Job market's shifting tide may change the rules.
feed.summary: A cooling job market is empowering employers to increasingly mandate a return to the office, shifting the bargaining power from employees. While many businesses still struggle to find workers, this trend is expected to accelerate, potentially leading to layoffs for those who resist office mandates. Some companies are even using return-to-office policies as a way to reduce headcount without official layoffs, as non-compliant employees may choose to quit.

feed.title: Tesla Sales Rise, but Revenue Still Lower Than Expected
feed.summary: Tesla Inc. reported third-quarter revenue of $21.5 billion, falling short of analysts' estimates despite a 56% rise in sales. The company's shares declined as CEO Elon Musk acknowledged economic headwinds impacting demand and delivery challenges, though he remained confident about Q4 performance and future growth targets. Tesla is also pursuing a share buyback and building a lithium refinery to secure its supply chain.

feed.title: Astroworld victim’s family settles lawsuits against Travis Scott and Live Nation
feed.summary: The family of Axel Acosta, one of the 10 victims who died during the Astroworld Festival crowd crush, has settled lawsuits against Travis Scott, Live Nation, and other defendants. The terms of the settlement are confidential. Acosta's family had initially sought $750 million in damages, and this settlement is part of approximately 500 lawsuits filed following the tragic event where 10 people died and hundreds were injured.

feed.title: Battle with Bots Prompts Mass Purge of Amazon, Apple Employee Accounts on LinkedIn
feed.summary: LinkedIn experienced a significant purge of fake employee accounts for companies like Amazon and Apple, with some firms seeing a 33% to 50% drop in listed employees overnight. The purges are believed to be LinkedIn's attempt to combat the proliferation of AI-generated profiles and fraudulent activity, including "pig butchering" scams and identity theft. This event highlights a larger problem of bot accounts and raises questions about the accuracy of LinkedIn's employee statistics and its broader impact on the platform's reliability for hiring and networking.

feed.title: The Dominos Theory of Cybersecurity
feed.summary: FTI Cybersecurity expert Greg Berkin applies lessons from domino toppling to effective cybersecurity strategies. He outlines three key theories: understanding the unique nature of each organization's digital footprint, recognizing that every component of a security system counts, and preparing for contingencies with alternative routes and backups. Berkin emphasizes that a collaborative, comprehensive approach is crucial for building robust cybersecurity defenses.

feed.title: Garmin introduces new functionality to the Morning Report
feed.summary: Garmin has released an Alpha update for Fenix 7, Epix 2, Enduro 2, and Quatix 7 users, introducing new features for the Morning Report and Run Power. The update allows customization of Morning Report items and adds Calendar and Step goal info, while Run Power benefits from alerts, new data fields, and wrist-based measurement. This Alpha version also includes various fixes and system enhancements, with a public release expected after beta testing.

feed.title: Taylor Swift’s ‘Midnights’ is the priciest digital album Tencent has sold
feed.summary: Taylor Swift's "Midnights," priced at 35 yuan ($4.83), has become the priciest digital album sold by Tencent's QQ Music, selling nearly 200,000 copies within a day of its release. This high pricing suggests either increased production costs or a growing willingness among Chinese users to pay for online music. The article also notes the weakening of Tencent's exclusive licensing deals due to regulatory crackdowns, allowing Swift's album to be available on rival platforms like NetEase Cloud Music.

feed.title: Apple earnings: What do the iPhone production reports really mean?
feed.summary: Ahead of its fiscal fourth-quarter earnings report, Apple is facing investor scrutiny over reports of iPhone production cutbacks, sparking concerns about demand in an inflationary environment. While Apple did not increase prices on its latest iPhone models, their high cost raises questions about consumer spending habits. Wall Street will be closely watching the earnings report for insights into inflation's impact on Apple device sales and the company's outlook for the holiday season.

feed.title: RAGNAROK ARENA: THE NEWEST RPG MOBILE GAME BY GRAVITY GAME HUB IS COMING SOON
feed.summary: Gravity Game Hub (GGH) PTE.LTD, a subsidiary of GRAVITY Co., Ltd., announced the upcoming release of their new mobile game, Ragnarok Arena, in late Q4 2022. This RPG, strategy, and idle game combines classic Ragnarok Online elements with new features like monster deployment and guild wars, and will be showcased at ESGS 2022. The game will be available across various regions on Apple and Google stores, and players can pre-register soon.

feed.title: 7 Virtual Reality Stocks to Buy to Bet on the Coming VR Boom
feed.summary: The virtual reality market is projected to grow significantly to over $227 billion by 2029, presenting a massive opportunity for investors, especially as many top VR stocks are currently oversold. This article highlights seven virtual reality stocks to consider, including the ProShares Metaverse ETF (VERS) for diversification, Roblox (RBLX) as a leading social metaverse platform, and tech giants like Microsoft (MSFT) and Apple (AAPL) that are heavily investing in VR/AR. Other recommendations include Nvidia (NVDA) for its Omniverse platform, Matterport (MTTR) for its exposure to "digital twins," and Unity Software (U) which recently received a "buy" rating.

feed.title: Apple Reports Earnings Thursday and All Eyes Are on iPhone 14 Sales
feed.summary: Apple is set to report its fourth-quarter earnings, with investors keenly focused on iPhone 14 sales performance and any guidance regarding the December quarter. Analysts anticipate resilient iPhone sales despite macroeconomic headwinds, while also looking for improved supply chain management for iPads and Macs. However, concerns remain about discretionary spending affecting wearables and the lack of specific revenue guidance due to ongoing economic uncertainty.

feed.title: Best Buy Launches Exclusive Upgrade+ Program for Mac Laptops
feed.summary: Best Buy has launched Upgrade+, an exclusive financing program powered by Citizens Pay, designed to make Mac laptops more accessible and upgradable. Customers can finance a new Mac laptop over 36 months, with the option to upgrade to a newer model, keep their current device, or return it at the end of the term. The program aims to offer affordability and flexibility while also contributing to environmental sustainability by giving returned devices a second life.

feed.title: Samsung Elec to expand foundry business to tackle TSMC
feed.summary: Samsung Electronics plans to significantly expand its foundry business, particularly focusing on mature and specialty nodes, to challenge TSMC's dominance. The South Korean tech giant aims to more than double its production capacity for these chip types by 2027, targeting areas like automotive and consumer electronics where TSMC currently holds over 50% of the market share. This strategic shift comes as Samsung looks to increase its overall market share in the foundry sector, where it has historically lagged behind TSMC despite advancements in leading-edge technologies.

feed.title: PayPal Introduces More Secure Payments with Passkeys
feed.summary: PayPal is enhancing its security features by introducing passkeys as a login method, initially for Apple device users, to replace traditional passwords. This move aims to simplify the login process and reduce security risks like phishing and password reuse, which are common causes of data breaches. Passkeys, based on FIDO Alliance standards, offer a more secure and convenient way for customers to access their PayPal accounts and complete online purchases.

feed.title: Apple (AAPL) Hikes Music and TV+ Prices in First Increases Since Launch
feed.summary: Apple Inc. has raised the prices for its Apple Music and TV+ services for the first time since their launch, citing increased licensing costs. Apple Music for individuals will now cost $10.99 per month, while Apple TV+ increased by $2 to $6.99 monthly. This price hike makes Apple Music more expensive than rival services from Spotify and Amazon, potentially giving competitors an advantage.

feed.title: Apple Moves iPhone 14 Production to India
feed.summary: Apple announced it will begin manufacturing its latest iPhone 14 models in India, marking a significant step in the company's efforts to diversify production away from China. This move aligns with India's push for local manufacturing and is expected to boost India's smartphone market. Investors can capitalize on India's digital growth through ETFs like the EMQQ Global’s India Internet and Ecommerce ETF (INQQ).

feed.title: Opinion: This one stock stands out when looking at key data from the FAANG+ group
feed.summary: This article discusses the current landscape for tech-oriented stocks, noting that 2022 has been difficult for the sector. It suggests that smart long-term investors should look for bargains among quality merchandise, particularly within the FAANG group. The piece aims to identify one stock from this group that offers a good combination of low valuation and high growth, despite the challenging market conditions.

feed.title: Apple puts more ads in the iPhone's App Store
feed.summary: Apple has expanded its advertising inventory on the App Store to include new ad units on the Today tab and individual product pages, significantly increasing its ad presence. This move follows the introduction of new privacy features like App Tracking Transparency, which has been criticized by competitors for hampering their advertising businesses while Apple expands its own. Analysts estimate Apple could generate substantial revenue from these search ads.

feed.title: Corsair K100 Air review: Skinny keyboard, fat price tag
feed.summary: The Corsair K100 Air is a super-thin mechanical keyboard featuring Cherry's Ultra-Low Profile switches, offering a sleek design and satisfying keyfeel with short travel. While praised for its aesthetics, engineering, and feature set including wireless connectivity and RGB lighting, its high price of $279.99 and short battery life with RGB on are significant drawbacks. The reviewer concludes that while it showcases impressive tech, it's too niche and expensive for most gamers and everyday users, suggesting the switches are better suited for gaming laptops.

feed.title: Yelp Fusion brings Yelp's trusted content to Mailchimp users and platforms across real estate, travel and more
feed.summary: Yelp is expanding its Yelp Fusion program, integrating its local business content and reviews with Mailchimp to allow businesses to easily incorporate Yelp data into their email marketing campaigns. This expansion also brings Yelp content to various new platforms in the auto, travel, real estate, and health industries, enhancing local discovery for consumers. Key partners include Ford, Audi, Roadtrippers, Realtor.com, and MetLife.

feed.title: Target adds more Apple mini-shops
feed.summary: Target announced it has tripled the number of Apple mini-shops inside its stores. This expansion indicates a significant increase in the presence of Apple products within Target's retail locations. The announcement was made on Wednesday, October 26, 2022.

feed.title: Google Pixel Watch Review: A Break-Out First Effort
feed.summary: The Google Pixel Watch is highlighted as a strong Wear OS smartwatch that integrates well within Google's ecosystem, providing a solid alternative to the Apple Watch for Android users. While praised for its design, screen, and responsive software, it faces challenges with sub-par battery life, a complex setup, and the necessity of a Fitbit Premium membership for some key features. Despite these drawbacks, the review considers it a promising first-generation product for Android users, especially if sleep tracking isn't a primary concern or if a Fitbit Inspire is used concurrently.

feed.title: Lowe’s opens tech hub
feed.summary: Lowe's has opened a new 357,000-sq.-ft. tech hub in Charlotte, N.C., centralizing its technology development activities to support its goal of becoming the most customer-centric omnichannel retailer. The facility, designed for innovation and collaboration, will employ up to 2,000 tech professionals and focus on projects like its interactive virtual store models ("digital twins") and the "Measure Your Space" app feature. This move aligns with Lowe's commitment to leveraging emerging technology to evolve its brand and services.

feed.title: iShares S&P 100 ETF OEF
feed.summary: This article provides current pricing and key metrics for the iShares S&P 100 ETF (OEF), highlighting its stability and offering guidance on how to invest through eToro. It details that OEF aims to replicate the S&P 100, comprising 100 top US companies like Apple and Microsoft. The piece also includes a section on trending ETFs and educational resources for investors.

feed.title: Apple beats but comes up light on iPhone sales and services
feed.summary: Apple reported fiscal fourth-quarter earnings that surpassed Wall Street expectations for overall revenue and EPS, but fell short in key product categories like iPhone sales and services. Despite year-over-year growth in iPhone revenue, it missed analyst estimates, and services growth slowed significantly. Mac sales and "Other Products" like Apple Watch and AirPods were bright spots, exceeding expectations.

feed.title: How Farley's plan is going two years in as Ford CEO — and why 2023 is crucial
feed.summary: Jim Farley's Ford+ plan, initiated two years ago, aims to transform Ford into a leader in electric and digitally connected vehicles by focusing on scaling EV production, enhancing software capabilities, and leveraging the commercial vehicle business, all while improving quality. Experts believe 2023 will be a critical year for execution, as the company faces a competitive market, economic uncertainties, and persistent quality issues despite significant progress in its EV strategy and talent recruitment. The plan involves substantial investments in electrification, a split into separate combustion and EV units, and ambitious targets for profit margins and EV production.

feed.title: Chris Gondran of Home Bank - How to Protect Yourself and Your Assets from Cybersecurity Thieves
feed.summary: Chris Gondran, Senior Vice President at Home Bank, offers critical advice on protecting digital assets from cybercriminals, emphasizing strong password practices, vigilance against social engineering scams (phishing, vishing, smishing), and the importance of multi-factor authentication and data backups. The article highlights how human error is a major cause of breaches and directs readers to resources like ic3.gov, stopthinkconnect.org, cisa.gov, and haveibeenpwned.com for further protection and reporting.

feed.title: Exxon's Record-breaking Q3 Profit Nearly Matches Apple's
feed.summary: Exxon Mobil Corp reported a record-breaking $19.66 billion third-quarter net profit, driven by soaring energy prices, nearly matching Apple's $20.7 billion for the same period. The company's profits were boosted by its continued investment in fossil fuels and strong natural gas earnings, enabling it to exceed Wall Street forecasts and maintain its share buyback program while increasing dividends. Despite withdrawing from Russia, Exxon is on track to meet investment targets and increase production in key areas like the Permian Basin and Guyana.

feed.title: Arm Could Change Licensing Model to Charge OEMs Directly
feed.summary: Arm might change its licensing model by requiring OEMs to license almost all of its IP if they wish to use its CPU IP, directly challenging existing deals and potentially pushing chipmakers toward alternatives like RISC-V. This potential shift, revealed through Qualcomm's counterclaim in a legal dispute, would prevent mixing Arm's IP with other companies' designs (e.g., AMD GPUs in Samsung chips). The change is expected to take effect in 2025, with exemptions for long-term licensees like NVIDIA and Apple.

feed.title: 3 Technology Stocks With 3%+ Yields to Buy Today
feed.summary: This article identifies three technology stocks—Cisco (CSCO), Skyworks Solutions (SWKS), and Texas Instruments (TXN)—that offer dividend yields of over 3% and strong total return potential. Despite a challenging year for the tech sector, these companies are highlighted for their reasonable valuations, robust business models, and prospects for double-digit annual returns through a combination of earnings growth, dividend income, and multiple expansion.

feed.title: Industry Voice: As Market Dynamics Pivot, Alpha Opportunities Are Set to Improve
feed.summary: The US equity market has seen challenges for active managers due to low volatility, market concentration, and reliance on macro factors and momentum. Historically, however, such extreme market characteristics have often led to improved opportunities for active stock picking and outperformance against passive strategies as these conditions normalize. The article suggests that current market dynamics indicate a potential shift where active management could find more avenues for generating alpha.

feed.title: Plaintiffs File Amended Complaint in Hard Drive Component Antitrust Suit
feed.summary: Several dozen plaintiffs have filed a third amended class action complaint against ten hard disk drive (HDD) suspension assembly manufacturers from China, Japan, and Thailand. The suit alleges a global conspiracy to fix prices and allocate market shares for HDD suspension assemblies, which are vital components in electronic storage devices. The plaintiffs seek to recover damages for increased prices paid by consumers during the class period, leveraging previous guilty pleas secured by the Department of Justice against some defendants.

feed.title: Tech investors think they know what will turn around Meta after a 72% implosion this year: Less Mark Zuckerberg
feed.summary: Tech investors believe Meta Platforms Inc. could recover from its 72% stock implosion if CEO Mark Zuckerberg steps back, as his focus on the metaverse is seen as a drain on earnings. Despite his strong control due to the company's capital structure, some investors view the stock as "dirt cheap" and believe it could significantly rise with better management focus on shareholders. Others defend Zuckerberg's long-term vision, citing his past successes and patience.

feed.title: ESG Indexes Lifted by Carbon-Efficient Firms but Underperformed
feed.summary: MSCI ACWI ESG Indexes underperformed during the first three quarters of 2022 despite generally outperforming from 2013-2021. While carbon-efficient firms positively contributed to performance, exclusions from the MSCI ACWI ESG Leaders Index due to ESG controversies and significant negative contributions from specific stocks like Apple and Amazon primarily drove the underperformance. Industry factors, particularly energy and aerospace and defense, saw a reversal in performance but played a lesser role in the overall ESG index underperformance compared to stock-specific exclusions and dollar strengthening.

feed.title: 「Amazfit T-Rex 2」を初めてのスマートウォッチに選んだ結果……アウトドアで大活躍！　を期待してまずは自宅で準備編 - Fav-Log by ITmedia
feed.summary: The article reviews the Amazfit T-Rex 2 smartwatch, an outdoor-focused device from Zepp Health Corporation, highlighting its robust features like dual-band 5-satellite positioning, 24-day battery life, and various navigation functions. The author, a first-time smartwatch user, details the challenging initial setup process involving language settings and app synchronization, which eventually led to a successful update and customization. Despite initial hurdles, the author anticipates the watch's utility for outdoor activities and appreciates its large display and durable design.

feed.title: Alleged Galaxy S23 pre-order poster reveals late 2022 announcement date
feed.summary: An alleged pre-order poster for the Samsung Galaxy S23 series, initially suggesting a December 23 announcement and January 6 release, was later deemed a production from a telecommunication company agency. While the poster's authenticity regarding dates is unconfirmed, it displayed designs consistent with earlier S23 renders showing a camera module similar to the S22 Ultra. The article discusses potential reasons for an earlier launch than previous models, such as lower foldable sales and recent competitor flagship releases, and highlights rumored features like the Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 processor, a 200MP camera for the Ultra model, and potentially larger batteries for the S23 and S23 Plus.

feed.title: Stuffcool launches 67W dual Type-C port GaN charger ‘Neo 67’ at Rs 4499
feed.summary: Stuffcool has launched the Neo 67, which it claims is India's smallest 67W dual Type-C port GaN charger. Priced at Rs 4499, this charger utilizes PowiGaN technology and is capable of fast-charging various devices including MacBooks, iPhones, and Samsung phones. The charger offers 67W output from a single port or optimized power delivery when both ports are used simultaneously.

feed.title: Mark Lee appointed new head of sales and president of Apple Korea
feed.summary: Mark Lee, former country president of Applied Materials Korea, has been appointed as the new head of sales and president of Apple Korea. He joined Apple Korea in mid-October, filling a vacancy left open for a year. Lee faces challenges related to Apple Korea's in-app payment policy, which is currently under scrutiny by the Fair Trade Commission.

feed.title: Former Apple, Inc. employee defrauds computer giant of $17 million, US attorney's office reports
feed.summary: Dhirendra Prasad, a former Apple employee, pleaded guilty to defrauding Apple of over $17 million and the IRS of $1.8 million through schemes involving kickbacks, inflated invoices, and material theft. Two other company owners were also involved and pleaded guilty. Prasad faces significant prison time and has already had assets seized by the government.

feed.title: World's largest iPhone assembly plant under new Covid lockdown in China
feed.summary: The world's largest iPhone assembly plant, located in China's Zhengzhou Airport Economy Zone and owned by Foxconn, has been placed under a seven-day COVID lockdown. This lockdown comes as Apple faces supply constraints for its high-end iPhone 14 Pro models and continues to rely heavily on China for production, despite efforts to diversify its supply chain. The factory is imposing "silent management measures" to combat a new wave of COVID-19 cases in the region.

feed.title: Comcast, Charter brand national streaming joint venture as 'Xumo'
feed.summary: Comcast and Charter Communications have named their national streaming joint venture "Xumo". The venture will see Xumo-branded 4K smart TVs and streaming devices, including a rebranded Comcast Flex and XClass TV, released in late 2023. This move positions Xumo to compete with other major streaming platforms, with Charter contributing an initial $900 million to the venture.

feed.title: The anti-ESG funds exploiting the US political divide
feed.summary: The article discusses the rise of anti-ESG funds in the US, which are exploiting the political divide by offering investments that prioritize shareholder value over socially responsible agendas. These funds, such as Strive Asset Management and ACVF, cater to investors who believe ESG investing is politically motivated and detrimental to financial returns. Despite their growing media attention, experts question their overall impact on corporate behavior and capital movement compared to traditional large asset managers.

feed.title: Best Buy Co. Inc. stock underperforms Wednesday when compared to competitors
feed.summary: Best Buy Co. Inc. (BBY) stock fell 4.17% on Wednesday, closing at $66.01, underperforming the broader market. This drop ended a three-day winning streak and left the stock $75.96 below its 52-week high. Competitors like Apple Inc. (AAPL) and Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN) also saw declines, while Walmart Inc. (WMT) experienced a lesser fall.

feed.title: HP Inc. stock falls Wednesday, underperforms market
feed.summary: Shares of HP Inc. (HPQ) dropped 3.12% on Wednesday, closing at $26.98. This decline occurred during a broader market downturn, with the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average also falling. HP Inc.'s stock underperformed the market and several competitors, ending significantly below its 52-week high.

feed.title: KLA Corporation Announces Robert Calderoni as New Chair of the Board
feed.summary: KLA Corporation has appointed Robert Calderoni as the new chair of its Board of Directors, effective immediately. Calderoni, an independent director since 2007, brings over 30 years of technology industry experience. He succeeds Edward "Ned" Barnholt, who served as Board Chair since 2006.

feed.title: Apple now valued at more than Amazon, Alphabet and Meta — combined
feed.summary: Apple's market capitalization has surpassed the combined value of Alphabet, Amazon, and Meta Platforms, demonstrating its strong performance relative to other major technology companies this year. This achievement highlights Apple's dominant position in the tech industry, especially given that its stock has fared better than its peers.

feed.title: PYPL Earnings: PayPal Q3 2022 profit and revenue beat estimates
feed.summary: PayPal Holdings Inc. (PYPL) announced stronger-than-expected financial results for the third quarter of 2022. The company reported an 11% year-over-year increase in revenues to $6.85 billion, surpassing consensus estimates, and adjusted earnings of $1.08 per share, also beating analyst predictions. CEO Dan Schulman highlighted the strong performance and continued investment in digital payments and commerce, including a collaboration with Apple.

feed.title: Meta's rising capex is a response to TikTok and Apple; Cassava Sciences Files Lawsuit Against Perpetrators of 'Short and Distort' Campaign; A pleasant experience flying Spirit Airlines
feed.summary: This article discusses Meta Platforms' increased capital expenditures as a response to competition from TikTok and Apple's privacy changes, arguing these investments are crucial for Meta's adaptation and long-term success despite communication shortcomings. It also covers Cassava Sciences filing a lawsuit against "short and distort" perpetrators, with the author expressing skepticism based on past experiences. Finally, the author shares a surprisingly pleasant experience flying Spirit Airlines, highlighting cost savings despite extra fees.

feed.title: Why Apple raised the price of the iPhone, but not in the U.S. and China
feed.summary: Apple increased the price of its iPhone 14 series for many international buyers due to the strong U.S. dollar and currency headwinds, which significantly impacted the company's revenue growth. CEO Tim Cook noted that foreign exchange headwinds were over 600 basis points last quarter, preventing double-digit revenue growth. While the U.S. and China avoided price hikes, consumers in markets like the U.K., Japan, Germany, and Australia saw significant increases, a strategy Apple previous reversed in 2019 due to declining sales, though they report no such drop-off this time.

feed.title: Buffett’s Billion-Dollar Bungle, Apple’s Peeling Production & The Lidar Linkup
feed.summary: This article discusses several market events including Berkshire Hathaway's significant investment loss in Q3, despite strong operating earnings, raising questions about Warren Buffett's investment decisions. It also covers Blue Apron's poor earnings report, Apple's production slowdowns in China due to zero-COVID policies, BioNTech's surprising decision to raise sales expectations despite falling revenue, and the merger of lidar companies Ouster and Velodyne, highlighting consolidation in the self-driving car technology sector.

feed.title: Samsung to only use Qualcomm’s AP for Galaxy S23 series
feed.summary: Samsung Electronics will exclusively use Qualcomm's Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 mobile application processors for its entire Galaxy S23 series, scheduled for release in mid-February. This decision aims to address quality controversies, as previous Galaxy S models used different APs based on region. The move highlights Samsung's strategy to strengthen its premium market presence amidst a decline in overall smartphone shipments but growth in high-end device sales.

feed.title: ASCENSIA DIABETES CARE ANNOUNCES GLOBAL INTEGRATION OF THE EVERSENSE CGM SYSTEM WITH APPLE HEALTH
feed.summary: Ascensia Diabetes Care has integrated its Eversense Continuous Glucose Monitoring (CGM) system with Apple Health globally. This update allows Eversense users to view their glucose data alongside other health metrics within the Apple Health app, providing a more integrated and holistic approach to diabetes management. The company, in collaboration with Senseonics, is also working towards iCGM approval and integration with automated insulin delivery (AID) systems.

feed.title: Matter is just the first step towards the ideal smart home
feed.summary: The Matter smart home protocol officially launched, representing a significant collaborative effort by 300 technology companies to standardize smart home device interoperability. While it aims for a seamless user experience and a revolution in product-buying, initial adoption will involve a gradual transition focusing on specific device categories like lighting, HVAC, and door locks. Future stages will incorporate more device types, but the protocol still needs to address broader issues like user data security and privacy beyond just device interoperability.

feed.title: Jumia Technologies (JMIA) Announces Appointment of New Management Board and Acting CEO
feed.summary: Jumia Technologies (JMIA) announced a new Management Board and the appointment of Francis Dufay as Acting CEO, with co-CEOs Jeremy Hodara and Sacha Poignonnec stepping down. The company aims to refocus on core e-commerce fundamentals, reduce operating losses, and achieve profitability. Antoine Maillet-Mezeray has been elevated to Executive Vice President, Finance & Operations, and a search for a permanent CEO is underway.

feed.title: Apple warns of lower iPhone shipments as COVID curbs hobble China plant
feed.summary: Apple announced that it expects lower shipments of its premium iPhone 14 models due to significantly reduced production capacity at a Foxconn plant in China, which has been affected by strict COVID-19 restrictions. This reduction is anticipated to impact Apple's sales outlook for the crucial year-end holiday season. Foxconn, Apple's primary iPhone manufacturer, also revised down its fourth-quarter outlook and is working to quickly resume full production at the Zhengzhou factory, which has faced worker discontent and operational challenges due to China's zero-COVID policy.

feed.title: Earphone and Headphone Market Size to Grow by USD 20720.41 Million From 2022 to 2027, Customer Landscape, Vendor Assessment & Market Dynamics - Technavio
feed.summary: The global earphone and headphone market is projected to grow by USD 20720.41 million from 2022 to 2027, accelerating at a CAGR of 9.84%. This growth is driven by technological advancements for product enrichment and increasing demand for IPX-certified devices, though high prices of wireless variants pose a challenge. North America is expected to contribute 29% to this growth, with in-ear headphones being a significant segment.

feed.title: Federal Circuit Reins in Albright Again, Orders Quick Ruling on Apple’s Venue Transfer Motion
feed.summary: The Federal Circuit has once again intervened in a case overseen by Judge Alan Albright, ordering a swift decision on Apple’s motion to transfer venue. The court found that Albright's scheduling order, which mandated extensive additional discovery and re-briefing, constituted an abuse of discretion by unduly delaying the venue transfer ruling and forcing parties to expend resources on substantive matters. This decision reinforces the principle that venue motions should be processed expeditiously to avoid unnecessary judicial and party burden, with the CAFC issuing a similar order in another Apple case on the same day.

feed.title: ‘Matter’ kicks off with a bang in Amsterdam – with Silicon Labs in starring role
feed.summary: The new 'Matter' IoT standard launched in Amsterdam, featuring Silicon Labs in a prominent role and showcasing new smart home solutions. Silicon Labs, aiming to be "the Nvidia of IoT," predicts 40 billion IoT devices by 2030 and significant growth for smart home IoT due to Matter's interoperability. The standard, backed by nearly three hundred companies, aims to simplify smart home experiences for consumers.

feed.title: Intel loses bid to revive antitrust case against patent foe Fortress
feed.summary: A U.S. appeals court upheld the dismissal of Intel Corp's antitrust lawsuit against Fortress Investment Group LLC, finding insufficient evidence that Fortress's patent aggregation practices had anticompetitive effects. Intel had accused Fortress of stockpiling patents to threaten tech companies, but the court said Intel failed to show it paid higher royalties or was prevented from making products. This decision follows a nearly $2.2 billion patent verdict against Intel in favor of a Fortress-affiliated entity.

feed.title: Apple AirPods Maker Dives After Revealing Major Client Loss
feed.summary: GoerTek Inc., a major supplier for Apple's AirPods, saw its stock plunge after announcing the suspension of production for an audio product from an unnamed "major overseas customer," potentially impacting 2022 revenue by up to $456 million. The news has fueled speculation that Apple is the client in question, while GoerTek's rival, AAC, experienced a significant rise in its stock. This event highlights the challenges faced by electronics manufacturers amidst plateauing demand.

feed.title: Meta Layoffs Deepen Silicon Valley’s Jobs Losses
feed.summary: Meta Platforms announced it is laying off 13% of its workforce, over 11,000 jobs, adding to job losses across Silicon Valley's tech sector. This decision, influenced by Mark Zuckerberg's admission of misjudging pandemic-driven growth, has been praised by investors and has boosted Meta's stock. The layoffs will also severely impact H-1B visa holders and have global repercussions, particularly for Twitter's international staff.
feed.source: VOA - Voice of America English News
feed.source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News

feed.title: Lilly’s new diabetes management platform to integrate Dexcom CGM
feed.summary: Eli Lilly is launching a new personalized diabetes management platform called Tempo, which will integrate with Dexcom's continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) systems. The platform includes a smart button for insulin pens and an app to help patients manage insulin delivery and access personalized resources. This initiative represents Lilly's strategy to differentiate its diabetes products and counter competitive pressures through medical devices and a connected ecosystem.

feed.title: Lululemon names Isabel Ge Mahe to board
feed.summary: Lululemon Athletica Inc. has appointed Isabel Ge Mahe, Apple's VP and Managing Director of Greater China, to its board of directors. Ge Mahe brings extensive global experience in technology, retail, and product development, as well as deep knowledge of the Chinese market. This appointment supports Lululemon's strategic focus on international expansion, particularly in China.
feed.source: FashionNetwork USA - The World's Fashion Business News
feed.source_domain: FashionNetwork USA - The World's Fashion Business News

feed.title: Lululemon appoints Apple’s Isabel Ge Mahe to board
feed.summary: Lululemon has appointed Isabel Ge Mahe, Apple's vice president and managing director for Greater China, to its board of directors, effective immediately. Ge Mahe's extensive experience in product development, leadership, and the China market is expected to be invaluable for Lululemon's global expansion plans. She also serves on the board of Starbucks.

feed.title: Marketing Ethics – The Push and Pull in the digital age
feed.summary: This article discusses the ethical challenges in digital marketing, particularly concerning data collection via voice-enabled devices and social media. Author Uma Iyer highlights how AI and machine learning are used to target consumers through their digital footprints, raising privacy concerns. The article also compares data protection laws in the US, Philippines, and EU, and examines India's evolving stance on digital privacy, emphasizing the need for robust regulations and user awareness.

feed.title: As Retrans Revenue Slides, Broadcast Stations are Finding New Ways to Grow
feed.summary: Broadcast station groups are experiencing a significant shift as retransmission (retrans) revenue, once a major cash cow, tightens due to declining cable/satellite subscriptions and increasing network demands. In response, these stations are exploring new revenue streams such as political advertising, studio and real estate ventures, and diversifying into sports programming and content ownership. The article also highlights the potential of NextGen TV (ATSC 3.0) as a future revenue boom for local broadcasters.

feed.title: Innovator Equity Managed Floor ETF Holdings
feed.summary: This article details the holdings of the Innovator Equity Managed Floor ETF (SFLR), which was established on November 9, 2022. The ETF has

feed.title: Xperi signs second TV maker for TiVo operating system
feed.summary: Xperi has signed Shenzhen KTC Technology as its second TV manufacturer partner for its TiVo operating system, aiming to expand its presence in the smart TV market. This announcement follows Xperi's recent disclosure of quarterly earnings and its focus on the operating side of its business rather than consumer electronics. The first TiVo OS-powered smart TV sets are expected to ship in the first half of 2023.

feed.title: Apple hires former Facebook executive to lead its System Information team
feed.summary: Apple has hired Timothy Campos, a former Facebook executive, to lead its information systems and technology (IS&T) department. This move follows the retirement of Mary Demby, who previously headed the company's information systems for three decades. Campos's appointment comes at a time when Apple is also slowing down hiring in most areas outside of research and development.

feed.title: Tesla stock hits two-year low after boss Musk sells more shares
feed.summary: Tesla Inc shares have dropped to a two-year low after CEO Elon Musk sold $3.95 billion worth of shares. This sale, coupled with concerns about Musk's distraction due to his Twitter acquisition, has intensified investor jitters. Analysts are questioning Musk's credibility and urging him to refocus on Tesla amid increasing competition and production issues.

feed.title: Innovator Equity Managed Floor ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:SFLR) $SFLR
feed.summary: The Innovator Equity Managed Floor ETF (SFLR) is an ETF that aims to provide capital appreciation by investing in large-cap US stocks while utilizing a laddered options strategy to hedge downside risk, targeting a maximum 10% loss on a rolling 12-month basis. Launched on November 8, 2022, the fund has $1.68 billion in assets under management and a net expense ratio of 0.89%. As of early May 2026, its stock had increased by 2.2% year-to-date and its top holdings include major tech companies like NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft.

feed.title: CVS Using Omnichannel Approach to Take Behavioral Health Beyond the ‘Care-as-Usual Experience’
feed.summary: CVS Health is adopting an "omnichannel" strategy for behavioral health, combining in-person, virtual, and digital care options to meet evolving patient demands. This approach seeks to move beyond the traditional "care-as-usual" experience, offering greater convenience and accessibility. While aiming for integrated and holistic care, experts also caution against potential fragmentation if not executed correctly.

feed.title: Apple hires former Facebook exec to lead its information systems and technology
feed.summary: Apple has appointed Timothy Campos, a former Facebook executive, to head its information systems and technology (IS&T) department, replacing Mary Demby who is retiring. Campos previously worked at Facebook in a similar role and co-founded a calendar app acquired by Salesforce. This hiring occurs while Apple is experiencing other executive departures and has paused most hiring due to economic uncertainties.

feed.title: CBS Lines Up Mariah Carey Christmas Special for December 20 | Next TV | Broadcasting+Cable
feed.summary: CBS will air a two-hour concert special titled "Mariah Carey: Merry Christmas to All!" on December 20, featuring the singer performing holiday hits from Madison Square Garden. The event will also stream on Paramount Plus and air on MTV after its premiere. Mariah Carey is noted as the best-selling female artist of all time, with over 200 million albums sold.

feed.title: Apple hires former Facebook exec as its new chief information officer
feed.summary: Apple has appointed Timothy Campos, a startup founder and former Facebook executive, as its new Chief Information Officer. He will lead the Information Systems and Technology (IS&T) department, which is responsible for the infrastructure supporting Apple's online services, customer support, and website. This hire follows the retirements of Mary Demby, the current information chief, and her deputy, David Smoley.

feed.title: Ragnarok Arena pre-registration rewards revealed by Gravity Game Hub
