feed.summary: Gravity Game Hub has announced the pre-registration rewards for its new mobile SRPG, Ragnarok Arena. The game combines RPG, strategy, and idle gaming elements, featuring a storyline with over 99 well-known monsters from the classic Ragnarok Online series. Players can earn in-game items and physical prizes through pre-registration milestones and Facebook social media goals.

feed.title: BlackRock-style money: The largest investment company will teach Ukraine how to fight the crisis. Why is this a good signal?
feed.summary: The Ministry of Economy of Ukraine signed a memorandum of understanding with BlackRock, the world's largest investment company, for advisory services on creating an investment platform to attract private capital for Ukraine's recovery. This cooperation signifies a potential shift for Ukraine from an emerging market to an "economic tiger" post-war, with BlackRock offering expertise in strategic investments, credit sector improvements, and long-term capital preservation. The article emphasizes that while this collaboration is a good signal, real results hinge on full cooperation from both sides and structural reforms within Ukraine to avoid another financial crisis.

feed.title: Reza Partners with Perfect Corp. to Enable Virtual Try On of Jewelry
feed.summary: French jewelry brand Reza has collaborated with Perfect Corp. to integrate AI and AR virtual try-on technology into its new eCommerce website. This technology, featuring Perfect Corp.'s AgileHand, allows customers to virtually try on rings, earrings, and bracelets in real-time digital renderings. The partnership aims to enhance the luxury accessories shopping experience by providing an immersive and impactful virtual try-on solution.

feed.title: Dow pops 1,200 points, S&P 500 jumps 5% in biggest rally in two years after light inflation report
feed.summary: Stocks experienced their largest rally since 2020 following an October consumer price index report that suggested inflation might have peaked. The Dow Jones Industrial Average surged 1,201.43 points (3.7%), the S&P 500 rose 5.54%, and the Nasdaq Composite jumped 7.35%. This market reaction was driven by a lower-than-expected CPI reading, leading traders to anticipate a less aggressive stance from the Federal Reserve regarding interest rate hikes.

feed.title: Salesforce Accessibility Execs Derek Featherstone, Catherine Nichols Talk Disability Inside And Out Of The Company In New Interview
feed.summary: Salesforce accessibility executives Derek Featherstone and Catherine Nichols discuss the company's commitment to disability inclusion, both internally and externally. They highlight the collaborative efforts within the tech industry to promote accessibility, the role of Salesforce's Office of Accessibility and Abilityforce ERG, and the ethical component embedded in their product development. The executives emphasize that accessibility is a continuous journey and a core value for Salesforce, aiming to create a thriving environment for disabled employees and expanding their user base globally.

feed.title: The $98 Roku Smart TV sold out in minutes, but you can still save big on a TV from Walmart
feed.summary: A $98 Onn. 40-inch LED Roku Smart TV from Walmart sold out within minutes as part of its "Deals for Days" Black Friday event. While that specific deal is gone, shoppers can still find other Black Friday savings, including a 55-inch LG 4K TV for under $300. Walmart+ members received early access to these deals.

feed.title: Sid Meier’s Civilization® VI: Leader Pass To Bring 18 New Leader Selections to the Critically-Acclaimed Strategy Game on PC, Mac and iOS
feed.summary: 2K has announced the Civilization VI: Leader Pass, adding 18 new playable leader selections to the strategy game. This pass will deliver content through six DLC packs from November 21, 2022, to March 2023, for PC, Mac, and iOS. The Leader Pass is included at no extra charge for new and existing Civilization VI Anthology owners on Steam and Epic Games Store, and will also be available for individual purchase.

feed.title: Bull vs. Bear: An Indian Century in India Equities ETFs?
feed.summary: This article presents a "Bull vs. Bear" debate on the investment case for India equities ETFs. While the bull argument highlights India's rapid economic growth, digital transformation, and favorable demographics, the bear perspective raises concerns about climate change impacts, political stability, and an uneven distribution of economic growth. The debate explores various ETFs and individual stocks relevant to investing in India.

feed.title: iPhone 14 Emergency SOS via satellite tested: Here's what it's like to use
feed.summary: This article details a test of the iPhone 14's Emergency SOS via satellite feature, showcasing its functionality and user experience. It explains how the feature works, from leveraging Apple's investment in Globalstar's satellite network to guiding users through emergency questions. The article also covers manually sharing locations via satellite through the Find My app and the availability of a demo mode for practice.

feed.title: BlackRock Ups Stake in Penny Stock MULN; Trims Holdings in AAPL and MSFT
feed.summary: BlackRock's latest 13F filing indicates an increased interest in penny stocks like Mullen Automotive (MULN) and Helius Medical Technologies (HSDT), alongside Alphabet (GOOGL/GOOG) and Tesla (TSLA). Concurrently, the firm reduced its holdings in major tech companies: Apple (AAPL), Microsoft (MSFT), and Amazon (AMZN), though these still remain its largest positions. This reshuffle reflects BlackRock's strategy to balance investments in established tech giants with riskier, high-growth potential penny stocks.

feed.title: Apple launches satellite-enabled SOS service in US and Canada
feed.summary: Apple has launched its emergency SOS via satellite service in the US and Canada, exclusively for iPhone 14 users, allowing them to send emergency alerts outside cellular network coverage using Globalstar's low-earth orbit satellites. The service will expand to France, Germany, Ireland, and the UK next month and is free for the first two years. This initiative is part of a broader trend of companies like T-Mobile, SpaceX, Lynk, AST SpaceMobile, and Huawei exploring satellite-to-phone connectivity.

feed.title: With AWAL and Platoon both acquired, Tencent-backed FRTYFVE is becoming an indie power player in artist services
feed.summary: FRTYFVE, a Tencent-backed "artist growth company," is rapidly gaining traction in the indie artist services sector by focusing on social marketing, streaming promotion, and artist financing. Unlike traditional labels, FRTYFVE prioritizes helping artists achieve 10 million monthly streams and financial independence, using a data-driven approach and a "12 Months To Make It" program that encourages prolific music releases and strong fan engagement. The company, which is profitable and has doubled revenue annually since Tencent's 2020 investment, aims to fill the gap left by AWAL and Platoon's acquisitions by major labels, positioning itself as a truly independent partner for high-potential artists.

feed.title: Best Buy Canada Launches Black Friday Deals
feed.summary: Best Buy Canada has launched its Black Friday deals early, starting November 18, 2022, both online and in-store. The company is encouraging customers to shop early due to strong inventory and a "Black Friday Price Guarantee" to ensure they get desired products and benefit from deals. Purchases can be made via in-store, online with various shipping options, or Quick and Easy Store Pickup, with several key deals highlighted.

feed.title: Revealed: Just one in four Trump-endorsed candidates using Truth Social actively
feed.summary: A Columbia Journalism Review investigation found that only 25% of candidates endorsed by Donald Trump for Senate, House, and gubernatorial elections actively use Truth Social. Despite this low engagement from even Trump-aligned politicians, the platform remains a significant hub for right-wing extremism and serves as Trump's primary communication channel after his ban from mainstream social media. The article highlights Truth Social's struggles with user attraction, advertising, and internal issues, alongside two federal investigations that jeopardize its future funding.

feed.title: Competition gets heated on Fungible’s DPU patch
feed.summary: Fungible, a data processing unit (DPU) startup founded in 2015, is facing intense competition from major players like AMD, Intel, Kalray, Marvell, and Nvidia. Despite developing impressive DPU chips and acquiring composability software, Fungible has struggled to secure major customers or OEM deals and recently pivoted away from composable systems to focus on its storage technology. The company reportedly tried to raise more funding unsuccessfully and is now in a precarious position amidst rumors of acquisition.

feed.title: Moody's closing its consulting business in China, laying off staff: Report
feed.summary: Moody's Corp is reportedly shutting down its consulting business, Moody's Analytics, in China and laying off over 100 employees across its Beijing, Shanghai, and Shenzhen offices. This move comes as China's zero-Covid policy and subsequent lockdowns are causing many multinational firms to reconsider their operations in the country. However, Moody's credit rating business will continue to operate in China.

feed.title: Opinion | How One of the Country’s Most Storied C.E.O.s Destroyed His Legacy (Published 2022)
feed.summary: The article discusses Jack Welch's regret over his choice of successor at General Electric, highlighting his belief that he prioritized charm over competence. Author William D. Cohan explores Welch's complex legacy, acknowledging his leadership while also criticizing his succession process. The piece delves into the debate surrounding Welch's impact on GE, questioning whether his focus on short-term profits ultimately harmed the company.

feed.title: Walmart's $228 50-inch Smart TV sold out in minutes, but you can go bigger
feed.summary: Walmart's popular 50-inch 4K Roku Smart TV, originally priced at $228, quickly sold out during a Black Friday sale. However, shoppers can still find a deal on a larger 70-inch VIZIO TV for $448. The article highlights the benefits of 4K resolution and the Roku Smart TV's features, encouraging consumers to upgrade their viewing experience.

feed.title: Taiwan’s TSMC eyeing advanced chips base in Arizona, founder says
feed.summary: Taiwanese chipmaker TSMC is planning to produce chips with advanced 3-nanometre technology at its new Arizona factory, according to company founder Morris Chang. This second phase of development would follow the initial 5-nanometre production, reflecting a broader global awakening to the importance of advanced chip manufacturing. Both the US and Europe are offering incentives to attract chip production to their countries.

feed.title: T-Mobile rolling out 4 lines, 4 iPhones, $100 a month plan
feed.summary: T-Mobile is reintroducing a popular holiday deal offering four lines of unlimited talk, text, and 5G data on their Essentials plan for $100 per month. Additionally, customers can get four iPhone 14 smartphones "on Us" by adding a line and trading in an eligible device. This offer represents a significant saving compared to similar plans from competitors.

feed.title: Sound United Parent Masimo Wins Big as Judge Finds Former Employee Stole Its Trade Secrets
feed.summary: Masimo, parent company of Sound United, won a significant legal battle against a former employee, Dr. Marcelo Lamego, who was found to have stolen trade secrets and used them to develop a competitive product and for patent applications. This ruling strengthens Masimo's ongoing lawsuit against Apple, Inc., which involves similar allegations of stolen technology and patent infringement related to Apple Watch features. The same judge presides over both cases, putting Apple in a difficult position.

feed.title: IPhone Pro models scarce in stores this holiday - Best Buy
feed.summary: Best Buy announced that higher-end iPhone 14 Pro models will be in short supply this holiday season due to production issues caused by China's zero-COVID policy. This scarcity is expected to negatively impact sales for both Best Buy and Apple during the crucial Black Friday and holiday shopping period, potentially pushing some sales into the next quarter. Despite strong demand for premium smartphones, analysts estimate a significant drop in iPhone sales compared to the previous year.

feed.title: Apple’s main iPhone plant boils over in violent protests against China’s tough COVID restrictions
feed.summary: Hundreds of workers at Apple's main iPhone manufacturing plant in Zhengzhou, China, clashed with security personnel over unpaid wages and fears of spreading COVID infection, stemming from tough restrictions in place for nearly a month. The violence, which saw workers protesting and clashing with white-clad guards, highlights the increasing economic and supply chain pressures caused by China's strict Covid Zero policy. Foxconn, the plant operator, acknowledged complaints about "work subsidies" and is communicating with workers and the government to prevent further incidents, as the situation underscores Apple's risks with centralized production in China.

feed.title: Samsung Secures 3-nm Advanced Chip Orders From NVIDIA, Qualcomm – Report
feed.summary: Samsung Foundry has reportedly secured orders from major firms like NVIDIA, Qualcomm, IBM, and Baidu to manufacture advanced 3-nanometer chips. This move, leveraging Samsung's gate-all-around (GAA) transistor technology developed with IBM, aims to position Samsung ahead of TSMC in advanced chip manufacturing and diversify the semiconductor supply chain amidst geopolitical tensions. Deliveries in large quantities are expected to begin in early 2024.

feed.title: Berkshire Hathaway says Warren Buffett donates shares to family charities
feed.summary: Warren Buffett has donated an additional 2.4 million Class B shares of Berkshire Hathaway, valued at approximately $759 million, to four family charities. This donation includes 1.5 million shares to the Susan Thompson Buffett Foundation and 900,000 shares split among foundations run by his children. Buffett has now donated over half of his Berkshire shares since 2006, with a significant portion previously going to the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation, though no new Gates Foundation donation was disclosed with this filing.

feed.title: HP Inc. stock rises Wednesday, outperforms market
feed.summary: HP Inc. stock (HPQ) rose 1.80% to $29.91 on Wednesday, outperforming the broader market which also saw gains in the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average. The stock closed $11.56 below its 52-week high and outperformed competitors like Apple Inc. and Microsoft Corp., though International Business Machines Corp. saw a slight decline. Trading volume for HP Inc. was significantly higher than its 50-day average.

feed.title: NASDAQ Trading - How to invest in NASDAQ (US100) index?
feed.summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide on investing in the NASDAQ (US100) index, detailing its structure, how it works, and reasons for its high volatility. It explains different trading positions (long and short) and identifies opportune times for trading, such as during periods of market panic or significant company announcements. The article also touches upon the use of CFDs and ETFs for investing in NASDAQ.

feed.title: What does Google’s ₹2,274 crore fine mean for Big Tech?
feed.summary: The article discusses the implications of the Competition Commission of India (CCI) fining Google ₹2,274 crore for anti-competitive practices, specifically regarding its Play Store and payment systems. It argues that such fines globally could signal the end of the unchecked dominance of major tech companies like Google, Apple, and Amazon, forcing them to re-evaluate their impact on innovation and society. The author suggests these regulations might ultimately save these companies from their own harmful habits.

feed.title: Foxconn apologizes for pay dispute that sparked factory protests in China
feed.summary: Foxconn, the iPhone assembler, issued an apology for a pay dispute that led to employee protests at its Zhengzhou factory. The company attributed the issue to a "technical error" in its hiring system and promised to honor the agreed-upon wages. Apple is also reviewing the situation to ensure employee concerns are addressed amidst China's "zero-COVID" strategy and related factory disruptions.

feed.title: Black Friday on track to top $9 billion in online sales for first time
feed.summary: Online sales for Black Friday were expected to reach a record $9 billion, potentially hitting $9.2 billion, despite recession worries and inflation. Adobe Analytics reported $7.28 billion in sales by 6 p.m. Eastern, with popular items including Apple smartwatches, AirPods, Funko Pop! figurines, and Pokémon videogames. This marks the first time Black Friday online sales were projected to exceed $9 billion.

feed.title: Apple's interest in Manchester United would mark a major escalation in the ‘streaming war’
feed.summary: Reports that Apple is interested in buying Manchester United could signal a significant shift in the streaming wars. Apple TV, currently a minor player, could leverage football's massive global audience to become a major streaming power. Acquiring a club like Manchester United would give Apple a crucial negotiating position for future broadcast rights, combining commercial opportunities with strategic content acquisition in the highly competitive streaming market.

feed.title: Dimensional U.S. Core Equity 2 ETF DFAC
feed.summary: This article provides details on the Dimensional U.S. Core Equity 2 ETF (DFAC), including its current price, key metrics, and how to invest in it through eToro. DFAC aims to replicate the performance of the Russell 3000 Index and holds investments in over 2,500 US companies across various sectors, with major holdings like Apple and Microsoft. The ETF, established in 2007 by Dimensional Fund Advisors, had net assets of $14.7 billion as of September 2022.

feed.title: Baxter overpaid for Hillrom • Sale of Weiss Memorial, West Suburban moves forward
feed.summary: A recently issued report confirms that Baxter International overpaid for its Hillrom acquisition, leading to a $3.1 billion impairment charge. Meanwhile, the sale of Weiss Memorial and West Suburban Medical Center is progressing, with Resilience Healthcare expected to take over operations. Additionally, the article highlights a rare lung-liver transplant performed at Northwestern Medicine and Illinois Attorney General Kwame Raoul's call for Apple to enhance reproductive health data privacy.

feed.title: iPhone 15 might feature "no buttons" - AppleMagazine
feed.summary: Apple is rumored to be planning to remove physical buttons from its iPhone 15 by 2023, possibly using solid-state haptic buttons instead. This move, supported by reports from Cirrus Logic and analyst Ming-Chi Kuo, could lead to cost savings, improved water resistance, and a sleeker design for future iPhones. The technology would function similarly to the Touch ID home button on older iPhone models.

feed.title: Hawley Demands Apple Condemn CCP COVID Crackdown, Reduce Reliance on Foreign Labor
feed.summary: U.S. Senator Josh Hawley sent a letter to Apple CEO Tim Cook urging the company to reduce its dependency on foreign labor in China and reshore production to the United States. Hawley also pressed Apple to explain its decision to limit the iPhone AirDrop feature in China, which has been used by anti-CCP protestors, and to address reports about potentially removing Twitter from the App Store. The senator questioned Apple's silence on the treatment of workers in China and sought details on the company's risk assessment and supply chain diversification plans.

feed.title: Fubo Sports Network Now Streaming on Amazon Freevee
feed.summary: Fubo Sports Network, the ad-supported TV network from FuboTV, has launched on Amazon Freevee, expanding its reach to over 155 million devices across various streaming platforms. The network, which reported its highest viewership in September 2022, now features original programming like “Airing It Out” with Housh and Scandrick and “The Young Person Basketball Podcast” with R.J. Hampton. This launch aims to provide more athlete-driven content to a broader audience at no additional cost.

feed.title: Lithium company CEO is ‘accelerating’ his U.S. investment plans, thanks to the Inflation Reduction Act
feed.summary: Kent Masters, CEO of Albemarle Corp., a leading lithium provider, is accelerating the company's U.S. investment plans due to incentives in the Inflation Reduction Act. The act's provisions, which favor North American manufacturing and materials, are expected to drive volume and profitability for Albemarle. This move highlights how new policies are creating significant opportunities for companies at the forefront of evolving economies.

feed.title: Android users in 12 US states cleared to sue Google Play
feed.summary: A California judge has approved a class-action lawsuit against Google regarding alleged anticompetitive practices in its Play Store. The suit, involving Android users in 12 US states and territories who made app or in-app purchases since August 2016, claims Google maintains a monopoly and charges a 30 percent commission, passing inflated costs onto consumers. This case echoes previous antitrust litigation against both Google and Apple, with the same law firm, Hagens Berman, involved, suggesting significant financial implications for Google.

feed.title: Apple TV's Lloyd Sam talks money — from a $50 dare to when he won’t pay extra
feed.summary: Lloyd Sam, an Apple TV MLS commentator, shares his financial perspective in an interview with Ally's "Money Clips" series. Sam discusses his early savings habits which led to accidental real estate development, his current savings goals for a Range Rover, and his refusal to pay extra for first-class travel. He also reflects on financial lessons learned from his accountant father, emphasizing value and hard work.

feed.title: President Joe Biden to visit TSMC plant being built in north Phoenix
feed.summary: President Joe Biden is scheduled to visit Phoenix on December 6 to commemorate a key milestone, the "first tool-in," at the Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) plant in north Phoenix. This marks his first presidential visit to Arizona and highlights the administration's focus on boosting domestic semiconductor manufacturing through initiatives like the CHIPS and Science Act. The TSMC facility represents a significant $12 billion investment in Arizona, aiming to reduce the country's reliance on foreign microchip production.

feed.title: President Biden coming to Arizona for semiconductor manufacturing plant visit
feed.summary: President Joe Biden is scheduled to visit the Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) plant under construction in Phoenix, Arizona, on December 6. This visit follows Arizona securing the chip plant and aims to highlight how Biden's economic plan fosters a manufacturing boom, rebuilds supply chains, and creates jobs in the region. The TSMC plant is expected to create 1,600 direct jobs and thousands more among suppliers.

feed.title: Spotify, Epic Games get ally in Musk in fight against Apple fees
feed.summary: Elon Musk has joined Spotify and Epic Games in criticizing Apple's 30% App Store fees, even suggesting Apple threatened to remove Twitter from its store. This stance strengthens the position of companies previously involved in antitrust complaints and lawsuits against Apple over its in-app purchase policies. Analysts are concerned that Musk's battle with Apple could deter Twitter users, as people are unlikely to abandon their iPhones.

feed.title: Spotify CEO renews attack on Apple after Musk's salvo
feed.summary: Spotify's CEO Daniel Ek criticized Apple in a series of tweets, accusing the iPhone maker of stifling innovation and hurting consumers by giving itself every advantage. This attack follows similar criticism from Elon Musk regarding Apple's in-app purchase fees. Spotify has previously filed antitrust complaints against Apple over its 30% charge, which it claims artificially inflates its prices.

feed.title: Vodafone Picks Dubber for UK and European Business Strategy
feed.summary: Vodafone Group PLC has partnered with Dubber, a Melbourne-based conversational intelligence and unified recording company, to deliver direct recording and AI services across its mobile networks for business customers in the UK and Europe, starting with Germany. This collaboration will leverage Dubber's platform to provide enhanced compliance and data insights across mobile and UC services, including RingCentral, Cisco Webex, and Microsoft Teams. The initiative aims to expand Vodafone's existing recording subscriber base and generate accretive revenue for Dubber by offering automated, self-service next-generation recordings and personalized customer experiences.

feed.title: TSMC to make higher-end chips in US: sources
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) plans to produce advanced 4-nanometer chips at its new Arizona plant when it opens in 2024, an upgrade from initial plans, following pressure from US customers like Apple. The company is also expected to commit to a second plant for even more advanced 3-nanometer chips. This move aligns with US efforts to bolster domestic semiconductor manufacturing due to supply chain disruptions and geopolitical concerns, though Taiwan intends to keep its latest technology at home.

feed.title: Samsung Galaxy S23 likely to adopt unbreakable smartphone screen glass
feed.summary: Corning Inc. has introduced "Gorilla Glass Victus 2," a new reinforced smartphone cover glass designed to withstand drops from one meter onto concrete. This new ultra-durable glass addresses the growing demand for stronger screens as smartphones become heavier and larger. It is highly anticipated that this technology will be featured in Samsung Electronics' upcoming Galaxy S23 series.

feed.title: Casetify teams up with Barbie on tech collection
feed.summary: Casetify has announced its first collaboration with Mattel's Barbie brand, launching on December 15. The limited-edition collection will feature a variety of customizable tech accessories, including phone cases for iPhones, Samsung, and Google Pixel, as well as accessories for AirPods, MagSafe wallets, and Apple Watch bands. Unique items include a Pin’It Case for Barbie charms and a Silicone Purse Case mimicking a Barbie doll bag, with prices ranging from 33 to 90 pounds / 38 to 98 US dollars.

feed.title: 40+ Spotify statistics 2026: SPOT stock, revenue and performance
feed.summary: This article compiles over 40 statistics about Spotify (NYSE: SPOT) as of late 2022, detailing its market dominance, user growth, financial performance, and stock behavior. It highlights Spotify's leading market share in music streaming, its revenue model primarily driven by premium subscriptions, and its significant user base despite competition from tech giants. The report also touches upon Spotify's stock performance, including its all-time high and low, alongside insights into artist earnings and global user demographics.

feed.title: After 18 years at Corning, an inventor of Gorilla Glass brought his expertise to Penn State
feed.summary: John Mauro, co-inventor of Gorilla Glass, joined Penn State six years ago after 18 years at Corning Inc., drawn by the university's research prowess and industrial collaborations. He aims to inspire students in materials science and is leading research on LionGlass, a sustainable alternative to traditional soda-lime silicate glass, which significantly reduces carbon emissions during production. Mauro is seeking industry partnerships to scale up and commercialize LionGlass to benefit both companies and the environment.

feed.title: How suite of dividend growth ETFs brings quality to the fore
feed.summary: In an environment of market volatility and economic uncertainty, CI WisdomTree ETFs offer a quality-focused core portfolio solution that has outperformed benchmarks. These dividend growth ETFs, managed by CI Global Asset Management, employ a unique methodology to identify companies with strong fundamentals, sustainable earnings, and resilient business models, screening for quality and growth metrics. This strategy aims to provide diversified large-cap exposure and long-term stability by avoiding overvalued companies and focusing on profitable dividend payers, proving particularly effective during periods of economic contraction.

feed.title: Apple sues AliveCor over patents as Apple Watch import ban deadline looms
feed.summary: Apple Inc. has sued medical-device maker AliveCor Inc., alleging that AliveCor's wearable heart monitors infringe on Apple's patent rights. This lawsuit comes as the U.S. International Trade Commission is poised to rule on whether to ban Apple Watch imports based on AliveCor's claims that Apple infringed its heart-monitoring patents. Apple contends that AliveCor's lawsuits are a response to its market failures and that Apple is the true pioneer of the underlying technology.

feed.title: MercadoLibre files complaints against Apple for 'anti-competitive practices'
feed.summary: MercadoLibre has filed antitrust complaints against Apple in Brazil and Mexico, accusing the tech giant of anti-competitive practices related to its app store. The e-commerce company alleges that Apple abuses its monopoly by imposing restrictions on the distribution of digital goods and in-app purchases, including requiring the use of Apple's own payment system and preventing redirection to third-party websites. MercadoLibre attempted to negotiate with Apple but ultimately decided to pursue legal action, while also noting that Google's app store policies are less restrictive.

feed.title: Check out the Polaris Slingshot 2023 Lineup
feed.summary: Polaris Slingshot has officially unveiled its 2023 lineup, emphasizing personalization and customization with new vibrant colorways and a host of accessory options. The release allows drivers to design their perfect ride with features like the RAM® X-Grip Phone Mount, an Excursion Rearview Mirror, and over 50 new color-matched accessories, alongside an enhanced infotainment system with Apple CarPlay®. The 2023 Slingshot models, including the S, S with Technology Package, SL, SLR, and R, offer various styling, performance, and comfort upgrades, with pricing detailed for each model.

feed.title: How TSMC’s AZ semiconductor plants will benefit U.S.
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) is investing $40 billion in Arizona for two semiconductor plants, with the second expected to open in 2026. This move is aimed at addressing national security concerns regarding the U.S.'s reliance on foreign-made advanced chips and is expected to create 4,500 direct jobs. The facilities will also incorporate environmentally friendly practices, including water recycling and sulfuric acid reclamation.

feed.title: Taiwan Semiconductor Plans to Build Second Fab at Phoenix Site
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) has begun construction on a second semiconductor chip plant in Phoenix, investing a total of $40 billion across both facilities. The first fab is expected to be operational in 2024 with 4-nanometer technology, while the second will feature 3-nanometer technology by 2026. President Biden highlighted these projects as a significant step in bringing manufacturing back to the U.S. and strengthening the supply chain, supported by initiatives like the Chips and Science Act.

feed.title: Apple Scales Back Self-Driving Car and Delays Debut Until 2026
feed.summary: Apple has reportedly scaled back its ambitious self-driving car project, codenamed "Titan," and delayed its target launch date to 2026. The company is now aiming for a less ambitious design, moving away from a fully autonomous vehicle without a steering wheel or pedals, which executives deemed unfeasible with current technology. The goal is to sell a consumer model by 2026 for under $100,000.

feed.title: TSMC triples Arizona chip plant investment, Biden hails project
feed.summary: Taiwanese chipmaker TSMC announced it would more than triple its investment in its new Arizona plant to $40 billion, marking one of the largest foreign investments in U.S. history. President Joe Biden praised the project as a move to bring American manufacturing back and strengthen the U.S. supply chain for semiconductors. The expanded investment includes building a second facility for advanced 3-nanometer chips by 2026, aiming to create 13,000 high-tech jobs and address semiconductor shortages.

feed.title: Sherman a contender for Finisar’s new $3 billion semiconductor project
feed.summary: Sherman, Texas, is under consideration for a new $3 billion semiconductor wafer fabrication plant by Finisar, a key Apple supplier. The project would create 700 jobs and produce silicon wafers for electric vehicles. Sherman is competing with other company-owned sites globally, and its bid for tax breaks through a Chapter 313 agreement is crucial for its competitiveness, despite the program's impending expiration.

feed.title: Apple is sued by women who say AirTag lets stalkers track victims
feed.summary: Two women have filed a class-action lawsuit against Apple, alleging that its AirTag devices facilitate stalking. They claim Apple has failed to protect individuals from unwanted tracking, despite marketing the device as "stalker proof." The lawsuit seeks unspecified damages for iOS and Android users tracked by AirTags or at risk due to Apple's alleged negligence, citing cases where AirTags were linked to tracking and even murders.

feed.title: Uber Launches Robotaxis but Driverless Fleet Is ‘Long Time’ Away
feed.summary: Uber has launched its first robotaxi service in Las Vegas, partnering with Motional, an autonomous driving joint venture between Hyundai Motor Co. and Aptiv Plc. This move reaffirms Uber's commitment to self-driving technology despite a fading hype around autonomous vehicles and other companies scaling back their efforts. Uber's global head of autonomous mobility, Noah Zych, stated that a hybrid network of driverless cars and human drivers is expected for a very long time.

feed.title: Amazon.com Inc. stock outperforms market on strong trading day
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN) shares rose by 0.24% to $88.46 on a day when the broader market saw declines, ending a four-day losing streak. The stock's performance outpaced major competitors like Apple, Microsoft, and Alphabet. Amazon closed significantly below its 52-week high, with trading volume also below its 50-day average.

feed.title: Apple AirTags Have Become Weapon of Choice for Stalkers, Class Action Alleges
feed.summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Apple, alleging that its AirTags have become a "weapon of choice" for stalkers and abusers due to their "unparalleled accuracy," ease of use, and affordability. The lawsuit claims that despite Apple's assertion that the devices were "stalker proof," the company "heedlessly forged ahead" with their release, leading to numerous reports of people finding AirTags placed on their belongings. It highlights two instances of murder where AirTags were allegedly used to track victims and features accounts from two plaintiffs who were stalked using the devices.

feed.title: Apple to be TSMC US fab top customer
feed.summary: Apple Inc. is set to become the largest customer of TSMC's new factories in Arizona, as announced by Apple CEO Tim Cook. TSMC will invest $40 billion in the US fabs, including a second plant for more advanced 3-nanometer chips, with production slated to start in 2024 for 4-nanometer chips and 2026 for 3-nanometer chips. Other major chip companies like AMD and Qualcomm also plan to source chips from these new US facilities, highlighting the growing importance of domestic semiconductor manufacturing.

feed.title: Broadcom gives upbeat revenue forecast as server, data center demand booms
feed.summary: Broadcom Inc. projected first-quarter revenue above Wall Street estimates, indicating strong demand for its chips used in data centers and networking equipment. The company's focus on infrastructure for hybrid work models gives it an advantage over competitors. Broadcom also expects to benefit from the global 5G rollout and its venture into software.

feed.title: Dexcom G7 Receives FDA Clearance: The Most Accurate Continuous Glucose Monitoring System Cleared in the U.S.
feed.summary: Dexcom G7, the next-generation Continuous Glucose Monitoring (CGM) System, has received FDA clearance for individuals with all types of diabetes aged two years and older. This new system offers enhanced accuracy, a smaller all-in-one wearable, and quicker sensor warmup time, building on the success of prior Dexcom CGM technology. Dexcom anticipates launching the G7 in the U.S. in early 2023, aiming to provide a more convenient and effective diabetes management solution.

feed.title: TSMC Revenue Rises 50% in November Despite Covid Challenges
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) reported a 50% increase in revenue for November, reaching NT$222.7 billion ($7.3 billion). This growth occurred despite a challenging environment marked by declining consumer electronics demand and COVID-19 disruptions in China. TSMC, the world's largest contract chipmaker and exclusive supplier of Apple's Silicon chips, continues a strong sales streak amplified by pandemic-driven demand.

feed.title: Corning Confronts R&D Hurdles
feed.summary: Corning Incorporated is facing significant challenges due to a recent tax policy change requiring the amortization of R&D expenses over five years, making innovation more costly. This shift from full deductibility poses a serious financial burden, potentially costing Corning an additional $150 million in cash taxes in 2022 alone. The company is urging Congress to either reinstate full R&D deductibility or at least make it retroactive to 2022 and extend it through 2025 to preserve its ability to invest in growth and innovation.

feed.title: Will TNT Walk Away From The NBA?
feed.summary: Warner Bros. Discovery CEO David Zaslav's recent comments questioning the necessity of the NBA for TNT have sparked industry speculation about the future of their 33-year partnership. As the NBA seeks significantly increased media rights—potentially $50 billion to $75 billion—from incumbents TNT and ESPN, the possibility of tech giants like Amazon entering the bidding process looms. While an NBA-TNT divorce seems unlikely due to their deep integration and the network's commitment to "Inside the NBA," the evolving media landscape and WBD's cost-cutting measures could lead to a reevaluation of the relationship, possibly involving a split package with streaming services.

feed.title: Disqo names CFO
feed.summary: Disqo, a customer experience intelligence platform, has appointed Scott Henderson as its new chief financial officer. Henderson will report to co-founder and chief executive Armen Adjemian and will oversee finance work, strategy, sales enablement, revenue operations, and business systems. He brings over 20 years of experience from Salesforce and other leadership roles at companies like Apple.

feed.title: Consoles and Competition
feed.summary: This article traces the tumultuous history of the video game console market, highlighting the shifting dynamics between hardware manufacturers and third-party developers, from Atari's integrated system to Sony's third-party focus and later emphasis on exclusives. It critically examines the FTC's attempt to block Microsoft's acquisition of Activision, arguing that the FTC's concerns fail to understand the industry's dynamic evolution and Microsoft's innovative subscription-based business model, which offers a competitive alternative to Sony's platform-locking exclusive strategy. The author suggests that the FTC is trying to preserve an outdated model of competition and is missing the real antitrust issues concerning dominant storefronts.

feed.title: Music now has over 616 million paying streaming subscribers globally
feed.summary: Global music streaming services had 616.2 million subscribers by mid-2022, an increase of 17.6% year-over-year, according to Midia Research. While growth slowed slightly compared to the previous year, notably due to macroeconomic pressures and the maturation of Western markets, the industry remains resilient with significant contributions from emerging markets like China. Spotify continues to lead the market, though its share has gradually declined as competitors like Tencent Music Entertainment, Amazon Music, and YouTube Music gain ground.

feed.title: As Market Dynamics Pivot, Alpha Opportunities Are Set to Improve
feed.summary: The U.S. equity market is shifting from an environment favoring passive strategies to one that is more conducive for active managers. Historically, during periods of extreme market concentration, low volatility, and low dispersion of returns, active managers often underperformed. However, as these extremes unwind—driven by factors like slowing economic growth, inflation, and rising interest rates—opportunities for active stock picking and alpha generation are expected to improve significantly.

feed.title: IBM Remains Big Tech’s Disaster
feed.summary: The article portrays IBM as a struggling legacy technology company that has failed to keep pace with its Big Tech counterparts. Despite its historical dominance, IBM missed critical opportunities in personal computing and cloud computing, leading to a significantly smaller market capitalization and lower revenue compared to industry leaders like Microsoft and Apple. Wall Street analysts maintain a "Hold" rating, with price targets below its current trading price, suggesting a bleak outlook for the company's future performance.

feed.title: Sonos Trueplay Now Works With iPhone 14 Models
feed.summary: Sonos has updated its Trueplay feature to support iPhone 14 models, including the iPhone 14, 14 Plus, 14 Pro, and 14 Pro Max. Trueplay uses the iPhone's microphone to analyze a room's acoustics and optimizes the sound output of Sonos speakers, a feature that was previously unavailable for the newest iPhone users since their launch. This over-the-air update now allows all Sonos products, except the Port, Connect, and Roam SL, to be fine-tuned using iPhone 14 devices.

feed.title: ElectraMeccanica Celebrates Commissioning Of New Manufacturing Facility And Corporate Headquarters In Mesa
feed.summary: ElectraMeccanica Vehicles Corp. celebrated the commissioning of its new manufacturing facility and corporate headquarters in Mesa, Arizona, marking its official establishment as an American OEM. The facility is expected to produce up to 20,000 vehicles annually and create 200-500 new jobs, bolstering Arizona's manufacturing industry. This move supports the production of their flagship all-electric SOLO and SOLO Cargo vehicles, with the company emphasizing the importance of partnerships with local and state governments for economic development.

feed.title: SPDR Gender Diversity ETF Officially Changes Index, Name
feed.summary: The SPDR MSCI USA Gender Diversity ETF (SHE) has officially changed its name and underlying index to the MSCI USA Gender Diversity Select Index, effective December 8. This ETF, a pioneer in gender-focused investing with $208.65 million in assets, now holds 281 stocks. The new index emphasizes companies committed to gender diversity across all organizational levels and rebalances quarterly.

feed.title: System in Package Market Size, Industry Share, Forecast to 2034
feed.summary: The System in Package (SiP) market is projected to grow significantly due to increasing demand for compact, internet-based electronics, IoT devices, 5G-connected devices, and the miniaturization of electronic components. Key drivers include the adoption of SiP technology in smart wearables, smartphones, gaming processors, and graphic cards. The Asia Pacific region is expected to lead this growth, primarily fueled by the consumer electronics industry.

feed.title: SPDR Gender Diversity ETF Officially Changes Index, Name
feed.summary: The SPDR MSCI USA Gender Diversity ETF (SHE) has officially changed its underlying index and name to the MSCI USA Gender Diversity Select Index. This ETF, a pioneer in gender-focused funds with $208.65 million in assets, now holds 281 stocks and seeks to identify companies committed to gender diversity across all organizational levels. The index is sector-agnostic, with holdings weighted by market capitalization and gender diversity scores.

feed.title: Discovering diamonds in the rough
feed.summary: The insurance industry is facing a significant talent shortage, with a large percentage of the current workforce set to retire and a lack of interest from younger generations. To address this, MetLife organized the Hack4Job 2022 Virtual Hackathon, a global initiative to identify and recruit tech talent. The event, supported by various national digital players, focused on critical tech areas and resulted in job offers for successful candidates, with plans to expand the hackathon globally.

feed.title: Apple planning huge change as a result of European legal push, report says
feed.summary: Apple is reportedly planning to allow alternative app stores on its iPhones and iPads in Europe, a significant shift in response to pending EU regulations aimed at promoting competition. This change would challenge Apple's long-standing proprietary App Store model, which currently takes a 30% commission on digital purchases. The move comes as governments globally push for alterations to the company's business practices.

feed.title: Why Kodak Died and Fujifilm Thrived: A Tale of Two Film Companies
feed.summary: This article analyzes why Kodak, once a giant in the film industry, ultimately failed, while its competitor Fujifilm managed to thrive despite the digital revolution. It attributes Kodak's downfall to its inability to diversify beyond the declining photo industry and its misjudgment of the digital market's profitability. In contrast, Fujifilm successfully leveraged its existing technologies and pursued aggressive diversification into new markets like pharmaceuticals, cosmetics, and highly functional materials, demonstrating a more adventurous and adaptable corporate culture.

feed.title: Z-Wave hopes open-source will matter to the IoT
feed.summary: The Z-Wave Alliance has open-sourced its protocol and moved beyond a single silicon vendor to stay relevant in the IoT landscape, particularly against the growing momentum of Matter and Thread. While this could foster competition and reduce costs for Z-Wave chips, the challenge remains its ability to attract new chipmakers and adoption compared to newer, widely supported standards. Z-Wave-LR also presents a notable opportunity for growth in Industrial IoT and larger properties, offering long-range connectivity where Matter and Thread might fall short.

feed.title: Australia takes aim at Apple, Microsoft over child protection online
feed.summary: Australia's e-Safety Commissioner has accused Apple and Microsoft of not doing enough to combat child exploitation on their platforms. Following legal demands for information, the regulator found that neither company proactively screens for child abuse material in their cloud storage services, iCloud and OneDrive, nor do they detect live-streaming of child sexual abuse on video services like Skype, Microsoft Teams, and FaceTime. The Commissioner deemed their responses "alarming" and highlighted a "major step backwards" after Apple announced it would stop scanning iCloud accounts for child abuse.

feed.title: Masimo links passive health monitors to home stereos amid legal row with Apple
feed.summary: Masimo is expanding its passive health monitoring technology by integrating it with home entertainment systems via a software upgrade to Denon’s HEOS system and Masimo’s Home Health Hubs. This initiative allows personal health information to be shared with clinicians and comes amidst a legal dispute between Masimo and Apple over alleged theft of monitoring technology. The move also clarifies Masimo's recent acquisition of audio brands, as it aims to provide continuous health data (like oxygen saturation, pulse rate, and temperature) to users and facilitate telehealth sessions through existing home TV and audio setups.

feed.title: Want to Attract Top Tech Talent? Prioritize Their Needs
feed.summary: This article emphasizes that attracting and retaining top tech talent requires more than just competitive pay; companies must prioritize technologists' needs for growth, modern tools, and an inclusive work environment. It suggests strategies like clear communication during recruiting, investing in up-to-date technologies, and fostering a culture of acceptance and work-life quality. Ultimately, understanding and acting on these priorities will help businesses thrive in the competitive tech talent market.

feed.title: Warner Bros Discovery Global Brands and Experiences Announces New Global Consumer Products Leadership Team
feed.summary: Warner Bros Discovery Global Brands and Experiences (GBE) has announced a new global leadership team for its Consumer Products business, integrating the legacy Warner Bros. Consumer Products and Discovery Consumer Products divisions. This realignment aims to maximize long-term consumer engagement with the company’s major franchises and streamline global relationships. Pam Lifford, President of Warner Bros. Discovery Global Brands, Franchises, and Experiences, will oversee the new team, which includes Julian Moon, Robert Oberschelp, Preston Lewis, Samantha Bushy, and Peter van Roden in key leadership roles overseeing different regions and categories.

feed.title: WisdomTree Launches Quality Growth ETF, QGRW
feed.summary: WisdomTree Investments has launched a new quality growth ETF, the WisdomTree U.S. Quality Growth Fund (QGRW), which tracks a market-cap-weighted index focusing on growth and quality factors. This new offering aims to avoid speculative growth names while including firms with strong growth signals, similar to their dividend growth fund DGRW. QGRW has a 28 basis point fee and includes major companies like Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon in its top holdings, starting with $1.3 million in AUM.

feed.title: What Is HP Smart Friend a Complete Overview | HP® Tech Takes
feed.summary: HP SmartFriend is a subscription-based tech support service offering 24/7 access to experts for troubleshooting, maintenance, and setup of tech devices from any brand. It provides various plans, including individual, family, and a one-time fee option, covering services like software installation, network troubleshooting, virus removal, and PC migration. The service aims to save users time and money compared to traditional repair shops by offering affordable, reliable, and accessible remote assistance.

feed.title: Apple Scales Back High-End Mac Pro Plans, Weighs Production Move to Asia
feed.summary: Apple is reportedly scaling back its plans for the high-end Mac Pro, which features Apple silicon, due to changes in its chip and manufacturing strategies. The new Mac Pro is behind schedule, and the company is considering moving its production to Asia. This news comes alongside updates on Apple potentially allowing third-party app stores and sideloading, the release of iOS 16.2, and Apple Music integration in Tesla cars.

feed.title: As markets shift, big office campus near Apple won't expand until anchor tenant is landed
feed.summary: A large office campus in North Austin, 7700 Parmer, which has housed major tech companies, will not expand its current 1 million square feet of office space until an anchor tenant is secured. The owner plans for a mixed-use future with additional office buildings, residential, and retail, but is waiting for market conditions to stabilize. This strategy avoids speculative development in the current economic environment.

feed.title: NSO Pegasus iPhone Spyware Vulnerabilities Fixed by Apple - Detect & Prioritize Using VMDR for Mobile Devices
feed.summary: Apple has released iOS and iPadOS 12.5.5 and 15.0 to address critical zero-day vulnerabilities, some actively exploited by NSO Pegasus iPhone spyware. Qualys recommends immediate updates and highlights four key vulnerabilities, including arbitrary code execution flaws in CoreGraphics, WebKit, XNU, and ImageIO. Qualys VMDR for Mobile Devices offers tools to discover unpatched assets and issue remote response actions to ensure timely updates.

feed.title: Apple CIO steps down from Rackspace board citing new job duties
feed.summary: Rackspace Technology announced that Apple CIO Tim Campos resigned from its board of directors, effective December 12, due to new job duties and potential conflicts of interest. This change comes amidst a series of management restructurings at Rackspace and their ongoing recovery from a recent ransomware attack on their Hosted Exchange business, which impacted thousands of small- and medium-sized businesses. The company is actively focusing on recovering impacted email data and transitioning affected customers to Microsoft 365, while also making other executive changes.

feed.title: Webull Expands into Australia
feed.summary: Webull has expanded its retail brokerage services into Australia, offering U.S.-listed equities, ETFs, options, and fractional shares through its Webull Securities (Australia) Pty. Ltd. subsidiary. This marks Webull's third market in the Asia-Pacific region, following launches in Hong Kong and Singapore. The company plans to add local Australian securities and Hong Kong-listed securities in 2023.

feed.title: Tech short-sellers make $12bn betting against Tesla in 2022
feed.summary: In 2022, short-sellers profited nearly $50 billion from betting against tech stocks, with Tesla being the most profitable target at $11.7 billion. This trend reflects a market shift from bullish to bearish, driven by high inflation and rising interest rates, leading to significant stock price declines for major tech companies like Amazon, Meta, and Apple. Despite the substantial sell-off, some analysts predict continued opportunities for short-sellers as tech valuations remain relatively high compared to historical averages.

feed.title: Sonos Roam Review: A Portable Speaker That Is More Than Meets the Eye
feed.summary: The Sonos Roam is a highly portable and robust wireless speaker designed to integrate into the Sonos ecosystem, offering impressive sound quality for its small size. It features both Wi-Fi with AirPlay 2 and Bluetooth connectivity, seamless transition between the two, and automatic TruePlay tuning. While it excels as part of a Sonos system, it's noted that dedicated Bluetooth speakers might offer more value for those not within the Sonos ecosystem.

feed.title: Why I can't stop paying the 'Sonos tax'
feed.summary: The author, a long-time Sonos user, explains why he considers the money spent on Sonos products a "tax" he doesn't mind paying due to the exceptional product quality and multi-room audio experience. He details the Sonos system and its various components, showcasing how it caters to different audio needs and integrates seamlessly into daily life. Despite its cost and a few minor drawbacks like drunk friends hijacking playlists or occasional update quirks, the author firmly believes Sonos enhances the listening experience and is an essential part of his home.
